def src(nb,i): return ''.join(nb['cells'][i].get('source',[]))
li=[i for i,c in enumerate(L['cells']) if c['cell_type']=='code' and "12.7" in src(L,i) and 'INTERACTION' in src(L,i).upper()]
mi=[i for i,c in enumerate(M['cells']) if c['cell_type']=='code' and "12.7" in src(M,i) and 'INTERACTION' in src(M,i).upper()]

print('lens',len(ls),len(ms))

out=base/'_tmp_cell12_7_diff.txt'

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import json, difflib
from pathlib import Path
base=Path('/Users/charleslevine/Library/CloudStorage/Dropbox/1-Documents/00- Dissertation/0-Next_Chapter/Code_and_Data/New SQL and PY Code/Cursor Workspace PDE')
L=json.loads((base/'520_pipeline_cox_working.ipynb').read_text(encoding='utf-8'))
M=json.loads((base/'520_pipeline_cox_working_20Mar.ipynb').read_text(encoding='utf-8'))

def src(nb,i): return ''.join(nb['cells'][i].get('source',[]))
li=[i for i,c in enumerate(L['cells']) if c['cell_type']=='code' and "12.7" in src(L,i) and 'INTERACTION' in src(L,i).upper()]
mi=[i for i,c in enumerate(M['cells']) if c['cell_type']=='code' and "12.7" in src(M,i) and 'INTERACTION' in src(M,i).upper()]
print('live candidates',li)
print('mar candidates',mi)
# narrow to notebook_cell_logging 12_7
li=[i for i,c in enumerate(L['cells']) if c['cell_type']=='code' and "notebook_cell_logging('12_7')" in src(L,i)]
mi=[i for i,c in enumerate(M['cells']) if c['cell_type']=='code' and "notebook_cell_logging('12_7')" in src(M,i)]
print('live idx',li,'mar idx',mi)
li,mi=li[0],mi[0]
ls,ms=src(L,li),src(M,mi)
print('lens',len(ls),len(ms))
print('ratio',difflib.SequenceMatcher(a=ms.splitlines(), b=ls.splitlines()).ratio())
out=base/'_tmp_cell12_7_diff.txt'
out.write_text('\n'.join(difflib.unified_diff(ms.splitlines(), ls.splitlines(), fromfile='20Mar', tofile='live', lineterm='', n=2)),encoding='utf-8')
print('wrote',out)
PY
```

```
with notebook_cell_logging('12_7'):
    # Reload pipeline_config to pick up any changes made
    # to configuration and other .py scripts
    if CELL12_7:
        reload_pipeline_config()
        # Ensure histogram helper exists (safe if cell 12.7A was skipped)
        if 'plot_var_distribution' not in globals():
            def plot_var_distribution(series, label, bins=30, color='steelblue'):
                """Plot a professional histogram with summary stats."""
                if series is None:
                    print_v(f"⚠️ No data for distribution: {label}")
                    return
                s = pd.to_numeric(series, errors='coerce').dropna()
                if s.empty:
                    print_v(f"⚠️ No non-null values for distribution: {label}")
                    return
                mean_val = s.mean()
                median_val = s.median()
                p05, p95 = np.percentile(s, [5, 95])
                fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 5))
                ax.hist(s, bins=bins, color=color, alpha=0.75, edgecolor='white', linewidth=0.8)
                ax.axvline(mean_val, color='red', linestyle='--', linewidth=1.5, label='Mean')
                ax.axvline(median_val, color='black', linestyle=':', linewidth=1.5, label='Median')
                ax.set_title(f"Distribution: {label}", fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
                ax.set_xlabel(label, fontsize=10, fontweight='bold')
                ax.set_ylabel('Count', fontsize=10, fontweight='bold')
                ax.grid(alpha=0.25)
                ax.legend(fontsize=8)
                stats_text = (
                    f"n={len(s):,}\n"
                    f"mean={mean_val:.3f}\n"
                    f"median={median_val:.3f}\n"
                    f"p05={p05:.3f}\n"
                    f"p95={p95:.3f}"
                )
                ax.text(
                    0.98, 0.98, stats_text,
                    transform=ax.transAxes,
                    ha='right', va='top',
                    fontsize=8,
                    bbox=dict(boxstyle='round', facecolor='white', alpha=0.8, edgecolor='gray')
                )
                plt.tight_layout()
                plt.show()
        # === 12.7. INTERACTION EFFECTS (3D PLOTS) ===
        print_v("\n📊 12.7: Interaction effects (3D plots)...")
        int_act = "12.7: Interaction effects"
        int_timer = time_start(int_act, nest=1)
        if cph_full is None:
            print_v("⚠️ Full model required for interaction effects. Skipping.")
        else:
            # Use centralized interaction config from pipeline_config.py

            dist_cfg = DISTRIBUTION_PLOT_CONFIG if 'DISTRIBUTION_PLOT_CONFIG' in globals() else {}

            dist_min_bins = dist_cfg.get('min_bins', 10)
            dist_max_bins = dist_cfg.get('max_bins', 60)
            dist_color = dist_cfg.get('color', 'steelblue')
            # Load coefficient map once (for interaction sign checks)
            coef_map = {}
            coeff_files = [
                "full_model_coefficients_stdz.csv",
                "full_model_coefficients.csv",
            ]
            coeff_df = None
            for coeff_name in coeff_files:
                coeff_path = os.path.join(cox_results_dir, coeff_name)

                    break
            if coeff_df is not None and {"variable", "coefficient"}.issubset(coeff_df.columns):
                coef_map = dict(zip(coeff_df["variable"], coeff_df["coefficient"]))
            if not int_cfg.get('enabled', True):
                print_v("⏭️ Interaction plots disabled in INTERACTION_CONFIG")
            else:
                plots = int_cfg.get
...
```

import json,re
from pathlib import Path
base=Path('/Users/charleslevine/Library/CloudStorage/Dropbox/1-Documents/00- Dissertation/0-Next_Chapter/Code_and_Data/New SQL and PY Code/Cursor Workspace PDE')
L=json.loads((base/'520_pipeline_cox_working.ipynb').read_text(encoding='utf-8'))
src=''.join(L['cells'][76].get('source',[]))
for m in re.finditer(r'.{0,80}set_title.{0,120}', src):
    print(m.group(0).replace('\n',' '))

for line in src.splitlines():

```
cell index 78
# === 12.8. MODEL-BASED PROMOTION CURVES (FIXED X/Y) ===
with notebook_cell_logging('12_8'):
    if CELL12_8:
        print_v("\n📊 12.8: Model-based promotion curves (fixed X/Y)...")
        mb_act = "12.8: Model-based promotion curves"
        mb_timer = time_start(mb_act, nest=1)
    
        if cph_full is None:
            print_v("⚠️ Full model required for model-based curves. Skipping.")
        else:
            var_x = var_x
            var_y = var_y
            label_x = var_x_label
            label_y = var_y_label
    
            # Ensure variables exist and are in the model
            missing = [v for v in [var_x, var_y] if v not in df_full_model.columns]
            missing_model = [v for v in [var_x, var_y] if v not in final_model_vars]
            if missing:
                print_v(f"⚠️ Missing variables in df_full_model: {missing}")
            if missing_model:
                print_v(f"⚠️ Variables not in full model: {missing_model}")
    
            if not missing and not missing_model:
                x_series = df_full_model[var_x].dropna()
                y_series = df_full_model[var_y].dropna()
    
                if len(x_series) == 0 or len(y_series) == 0:
                    print_v("⚠️ Insufficient data for model-based curves (all NaNs).")
                else:
                    # Use low/med/high quantiles for fixed values
                    x_vals = np.quantile(x_series, [0.2, 0.5, 0.8])
                    y_vals = np.quantile(y_series, [0.2, 0.5, 0.8])
                    level_labels = ["Low", "Mid", "High"]
    
                    # Time grid for model-based survival curves (use durations, not absolute stop_time)
                    try:
                        full_durations = (df_full_model['stop_time'] - df_full_model['start_time']).astype(float)
                        t_max = np.nanpercentile(full_durations, 95)    
                    except Exception:
                        try:
                            full_durations = (df_pipeline_12_01_prepared['stop_time'] - df_pipeline_12_01_prepared['start_time']).astype(float)
                            t_max = np.nanpercentile(full_durations, 95)    
                        except Exception:                
                            t_max = np.nanpercentile(df_full_model['stop_time'], 95) 
                    t_max = max(float(t_max), 1.0)
                    time_grid = np.linspace(0, t_max, 200)
    
                    # Baseline: median for all other variables
                    baseline = df_full_model[final_model_vars].median().to_dict()
    
                    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(18, 10))
                    colors = plt.cm.Set1(np.linspace(0, 1, len(x_vals) * len(y_vals)))
    
                    idx = 0
                    for i, xv in enumerate(x_vals):
                        for j, yv in enumerate(y_vals):
                            row = baseline.copy()
                            row[var_x] = float(xv)
                            row[var_y] = float(yv)
                            X = pd.DataFrame([row])[final_model_vars]
    
                            try:
                                surv = cph_full.predict_survival_function(X)
    
                                # sksurv returns list of step functions; lifelines returns a DataFrame
                                if isinstance(surv, pd.DataFrame):
                                    surv_series = surv.iloc[:, 0]
                                    t_vals = surv_series.index.values.astype(float)
                                    s_vals = surv_series.values.astype(float)
                                    grid = time_grid[(time_grid &gt;= t_vals.min()) &amp; (time_grid &lt;= t_vals.max())]
                                    if len(grid) &lt; 2:
                                        grid = t_vals
                                    surv_interp = np.interp(grid, t_vals, s_vals)
                                else:
                                    sf = surv[0]
                                    t_vals = np.asarray(sf.x,dtype=float)
                                    grid = time_grid[(time_grid &gt;= t_vals.min()) &amp; (time_grid &lt;= t_vals.max())]
                                    if len(grid) &lt; 2:
                                        grid = t_vals
                                    surv_interp = sf(grid)
    
                                promo_prob = 1 - np.asarray(surv_interp, dtype=float)
                                label = (
                                    f"{var_x} {level_labels[i]} ({xv:.2f}) | "
                                    f"{var_y} {level_labels[j]} ({yv:.2f})"
                                )
                                ax.plot(grid, promo_prob, color=colors[idx], linewidth=2, label=label)
                            except Exception as e:
                                print_v(f"⚠️ Curve failed for X={xv:.3f}, Y={yv:.3f}: {e}")
    
                            idx += 1
    
                    ax.set_title(
                        "Model-based Promotion Probability (Cox)\nFixed X/Y values (1 - S(t))",
                        fontsize=12,
                        fontweight='bold'
                    )
                    ax.set_xlabel('Days from Promotion to CPT', fontsize=axis_sz)
                    ax.set_ylabel('Promotion Probability (1 - S(t))', fontsize=axis_sz)
                    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
                    ax.legend(
                        title='Fixed values',
                        bbox_to_anchor=(1.05, 1),
                        loc='upper left',
                        fontsize=lgnd_sz,
                        title_fontsize=lgnd_sz
                    )
    
                    plt.tight_layout()
                    save_path = os.path.join(
                        cox_results_dir,
                        f"model_based_promo_curves_{var_x}_vs_{var_y}.png"
                    )
                    plt.savefig(save_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
                    plt.show()
                    plt.close()
                    print_v(f"  ✅ Saved model-based curves: {save_path}")
    
        time_stop(mb_timer, action=mb_act, nest=1)
    else:
        print_v("⏭️ Skipping 12.8: Model-based promotion curves")


```

</tool-use>

_**Agent (default)**_

Here’s a plain-language read of **Cell 12.8** from your live notebook.

## What 12.8 is for

**Title:** “Model-based promotion curves (fixed X/Y).”

It takes your **fitted full Cox model** (`cph_full`) and draws **model-implied “promotion probability over time”** curves when you hold **two predictors** (`var_x` and `var_y`) at fixed levels, while everything else is held at a **single baseline profile** (medians of all other model variables).

So it’s **not** raw Kaplan–Meier by subgroup; it’s **what the Cox model says** about \(1 - S(t)\) under those counterfactual rows.

## What it does step by step

1. **Needs** `cph_full`, `df_full_model`, and `final_model_vars`. If the full model isn’t there, it skips.

2. **Uses** `var_x`, `var_y`, `var_x_label`, `var_y_label` — it checks they exist in `df_full_model` and are in `final_model_vars`.

3. **Picks three levels** for each variable from the data: **20th, 50th, and 80th percentiles** → labeled **Low / Mid / High** (so **3 × 3 = 9** combinations).

4. **Builds a time grid** from observed **episode lengths** (roughly 0 to the 95th percentile of `stop_time - start_time`, with fallbacks), ~200 points.

5. **Baseline row:** for every other covariate in `final_model_vars`, sets the value to the **median** in `df_full_model`. Then for each of the 9 pairs, sets `var_x` and `var_y` to those fixed quantile values.

6. **Predicts** survival with `cph_full.predict_survival_function(X)` and plots  
   **promotion probability ≈ 1 − S(t)**  
   (so the y-axis is “model-based chance promotion has happened by time \(t\)” in that Cox framing).

7. **Saves** a figure like  
   `model_based_promo_curves_{var_x}_vs_{var_y}.png` under `cox_results_dir`.

## How it relates to 12.7

**12.7** loops over interaction specs and, inside that loop, redefines `var_x` / `var_y` (and labels). **12.8** does `var_x = var_x` / `var_y = var_y`, so it’s really saying: **“use whatever `var_x` / `var_y` are in memory right now.”** In practice that’s usually the **last** interaction pair from the last run of **12.7**. If you skip 12.7 or run 12.8 in a fresh kernel without defining those names, 12.8 can error or use the wrong pair — worth knowing if you ever run cells out of order.

---

**Short summary:** **12.8 = one figure of 9 Cox-based “promotion vs time” curves**, scanning **low/mid/high** for two chosen covariates, with **all other covariates fixed at their medians** — a **model-based sensitivity** complement to the **3D interaction plots** in 12.7.

---

```
# **🔍 CELL 12: COX REGRESSION MODELS**
##### **Comprehensive Cox regression analysis using scikit-survival**
- **12.1**: Prepare data for Cox regression
- **12.2**: Fit static model (demographics only)
- **12.3**: Fit full model (static + time-varying covariates)
- **12.4**: Model comparison and signal ratios
- **12.5**: Competing risks analysis
- **12.6**: Partial effects plots
- **12.7**: Interaction effects (3D plots)
##### **Focus**: Modular Cox regression analysis with progressive saves for troubleshooting


=== cell 79 len 4126 ===
# **✅ PIPELINE SUMMARY**
## **Pipeline Stages &amp; Output Files**
1. **PHASE 1: Data Loading &amp; Basic Preparation**
   - &#96;df_pipeline_01_raw&#96; - Raw snapshot data
   - &#96;df_pipeline_02_base&#96; - Filtered base data (clean)
   - &#96;df_pipeline_03_base&#96; - Filtered base data with yg
2. **PHASE 2: OER Integration**
   - &#96;df_pipeline_04a_basic_metrics&#96; - Individual fwd/bwd metrics
   - &#96;df_pipeline_05_pool_means&#96; - Pool means/minus-means/sizes (fwd/bwd)
   - &#96;df_pipeline_06_pool_ranks&#96; - Pool ranks/pct/z-scores (fwd/bwd)
3. **PHASE 3: Variable Creation**
   - &#96;df_pipeline_07_time_varying&#96; - Time-varying variables added (includes prestige_unit, prestige_sum if from 502)
   - &#96;df_pipeline_08_combined&#96; - Combined time-varying + static variables
4. **PHASE 4: Advanced Filtering**
   - &#96;df_pipeline_09_filtered&#96; - Filtered dataset ready for analysis
5. **PHASE 5: Cox Analysis**
   - &#96;df_pipeline_10_cox_ready&#96; - Base Cox-ready dataset (time-varying survival intervals)
   - &#96;df_pipeline_10_5_cox_zscored&#96; - STandardized + interaction dataset for modeling/plots
   - &#96;df_pipeline_11_cox_analysis&#96; - Analysis dataset with selected columns for plotting
   - &#96;df_pipeline_12_01_prepared&#96; - Prepared data for Cox regression models (CELL 12)
## **Key Columns for Immediate Analysis**
- **TB Ratios**: &#96;TB_RATIO_FWD_*&#96;, &#96;TB_RATIO_BWD_*&#96;
- **Pool Metrics**: &#96;POOL_TB_RATIO_MEAN_*&#96;, &#96;POOL_MINUS_MEAN_*&#96;, &#96;POOL_TB_RATIO_RANK_PCT_*&#96;, &#96;POOL_TB_RATIO_ZPOOL_*&#96;, &#96;POOL_SIZE_*&#96;
- **Demographics**: &#96;yg&#96;, &#96;sex&#96;, &#96;age_cpt&#96;
- **OER Metrics**: All cumulative + pool metrics from &#96;assign_oer_to_snapshots_fast()&#96; and pool steps
- **Time-Varying Variables**: &#96;prestige_unit&#96;, &#96;prestige_sum&#96;
## **Completed Work**
✅ **CELL 7**: Unit classification logic completed (with fallback if not loading from 502)
- Creates &#96;prestige_unit&#96; and &#96;prestige_sum&#96; if not already present
- Loads UIC hierarchy data and creates unit lists
- Maps prestige metrics to all snapshots
✅ **CELL 10**: Cox data preparation completed
- Creates time-varying survival intervals with &#96;start_time&#96;, &#96;stop_time&#96;, &#96;event&#96; columns
- Handles competing risks (promotion, attrition, censoring)
- Preserves all time-varying covariates
✅ **CELL 11**: Cox analysis and plotting completed
- Enhanced plotting utilities ported from 513 (font scaling, reference lines, subtitles)
- Flexible configuration-based plotting system
- Kaplan-Meier and competing risks plots
- OER filtering support
- Division name plotting support
✅ **CELL 12**: Cox regression models
- **12.1**: Data preparation (✅ Complete)
- **12.2**: Static model fitting (✅ Complete)
- **12.3**: Full model with time-varying covariates (✅ Complete - uses interval-level data with dynamic covariates)
- **12.4**: Model comparison and signal ratios (✅ Complete - 4-panel visualization comparing static vs full models)
- **12.5**: Competing risks analysis (✅ Complete - separate models for promotion and attrition with coefficient comparison)
- **12.6**: Partial effects plots (✅ Complete - hazard ratio curves for continuous variables, signal ratios for categorical)
- **12.7**: Interaction effects 3D plots (⏳ Placeholder - ready for expansion)
```

```python
def set_cell_source(cell, new_text):
```

old54="""- **12.6**: Partial effects plots

"""

("""   - `df_pipeline_07_time_varying` - Time-varying variables added (includes prestige_unit, prestige_sum if from 502)

"""),
("""✅ **CELL 11**: Cox analysis and plotting completed

- Flexible configuration-based plotting system
- Kaplan-Meier and competing risks plots
- OER filtering support
- Division name plotting support

"""✅ **CELL 11**: Cox analysis and plotting completed
- Enhanced plotting utilities ported from 513 (font scaling, reference lines, subtitles)
- Flexible configuration-based plotting system (`PLOT_CONFIG` in `pipeline_config.py`)
- Kaplan-Meier and competing risks plots; optional CIF bar panels and metadata cards
- Paper-friendly defaults with optional legacy toggles (`show_plot_titles`, `show_plot_subtitles`, legend placement, CIF bar titles/x-labels, etc.)
- OER filtering support
- Division name plotting support
"""),
("""- **12.6**: Partial effects plots (✅ Complete - hazard ratio curves for continuous variables, signal ratios for categorical)
- **12.7**: Interaction effects 3D plots (⏳ Placeholder - ready for expansion)
## **Next Steps**
""",
"""- **12.6**: Partial effects plots (✅ Complete - hazard ratio curves for continuous variables, signal ratios for categorical)
- **12.6A**: Combined linear + quadratic panels (✅ Complete - minimal / full / TB-only / incremental / remove-one views for `base`+`_sq` pairs)
- **12.7**: Interaction effects (✅ Complete - 3D HR surfaces, contour + inset slice, marginal effect plots, distributions/correlation; driven by `INTERACTION_CONFIG`; plot titles respect `PLOT_CONFIG['show_plot_titles']`)
- **12.8**: Model-based promotion curves (✅ Complete - nine Cox curves at 20th/50th/80th percentiles of `var_x`×`var_y`, other covariates at medians; **uses `var_x`/`var_y` left in memory after 12.7**, typically the last interaction spec)
## **Next Steps**
"""),
("""7. ✅ ~~Implement CELL 12.6 (Partial effects plots)~~ - **COMPLETED**
8. ⏳ **Implement CELL 12.7 (Interaction effects with 3D surface plots)** - Ready for implementation
9. 🔄 **Test pipeline with sample data** - Validate end-to-end workflow
10. 🔄 **Refine filtering parameters in CELL 9** - Optimize based on analysis results
11. 🔄 **Integration testing** - Ensure 502 → 520 pipeline works seamlessly
12. 🔄 **Documentation** - Update overview files with CELL 12 details
""",
"""7. ✅ ~~Implement CELL 12.6 (Partial effects plots)~~ - **COMPLETED**
8. ✅ ~~Implement CELL 12.7 (Interaction effects: 3D surfaces, contours, slices)~~ - **COMPLETED**
9. ✅ ~~Implement CELL 12.8 (Model-based promotion curves at fixed X/Y)~~ - **COMPLETED**
10. 🔄 **Test pipeline with sample data** - Validate end-to-end workflow
11. 🔄 **Refine filtering parameters in CELL 9** - Optimize based on analysis results
12. 🔄 **Integration testing** - Ensure upstream prep → 520 pipeline works seamlessly
13. 🔄 **Documentation** - Keep overview / summary cells aligned with `pipeline_config.py` and new cells
"""),
]
for a,b in repls:

c79['source']=[ln if ln.endswith('\n') else ln+'\n' for ln in s79.splitlines(True)]

```
# **🔍 CELL 12: COX REGRESSION MODELS**
##### **Comprehensive Cox regression analysis using scikit-survival**
- **12.1**: Prepare data for Cox regression
- **12.2**: Fit static model (demographics only)
- **12.3**: Fit full model (static + time-varying covariates)
- **12.4**: Model comparison and signal ratios
- **12.5**: Competing risks analysis
- **12.6**: Partial effects plots
- **12.7**: Interaction effects (3D plots)
##### **Focus**: Modular Cox regression analysis with progressive saves for troubleshooting

--- tail 78 ---
hots
✅ **CELL 10**: Cox data preparation completed
- Creates time-varying survival intervals with &#96;start_time&#96;, &#96;stop_time&#96;, &#96;event&#96; columns
- Handles competing risks (promotion, attrition, censoring)
- Preserves all time-varying covariates
✅ **CELL 11**: Cox analysis and plotting completed
- Enhanced plotting utilities ported from 513 (font scaling, reference lines, subtitles)
- Flexible configuration-based plotting system
- Kaplan-Meier and competing risks plots
- OER filtering support
- Division name plotting support
✅ **CELL 12**: Cox regression models
- **12.1**: Data preparation (✅ Complete)
- **12.2**: Static model fitting (✅ Complete)
- **12.3**: Full model with time-varying covariates (✅ Complete - uses interval-level data with dynamic covariates)
- **12.4**: Model comparison and signal ratios (✅ Complete - 4-panel visualization comparing static vs full models)
- **12.5**: Competing risks analysis (✅ Complete - separate models for promotion and attrition with coefficient comparison)
- **12.6**: Partial effects plots (✅ Complete - hazard ratio curves for continuous variables, signal ratios for categorical)
- **12.7**: Interaction effects 3D plots (⏳ Placeholder - ready for expansion)
## **Next Steps**
1. ✅ ~~Complete CELL 10 (Cox data preparation)~~ - **COMPLETED**
2. ✅ ~~Port Cox analysis cells from 513 (CELL 11)~~ - **COMPLETED** (plotting with enhanced utilities)
3. ✅ ~~Add unit classification definitions in CELL 7~~ - **COMPLETED** (with fallback logic)
4. ✅ ~~Implement CELL 12.3 (Full model with time-varying covariates)~~ - **COMPLETED**
5. ✅ ~~Implement CELL 12.4 (Model comparison and signal ratios)~~ - **COMPLETED**
6. ✅ ~~Implement CELL 12.5 (Competing risks analysis)~~ - **COMPLETED**
7. ✅ ~~Implement CELL 12.6 (Partial effects plots)~~ - **COMPLETED**
8. ⏳ **Implement CELL 12.7 (Interaction effects with 3D surface plots)** - Ready for implementation
9. 🔄 **Test pipeline with sample data** - Validate end-to-end workflow
10. 🔄 **Refine filtering parameters in CELL 9** - Optimize based on analysis results
11. 🔄 **Integration testing** - Ensure 502 → 520 pipeline works seamlessly
12. 🔄 **Documentation** - Update overview files with CELL 12 details


```

import json
from pathlib import Path
path=Path('/Users/charleslevine/Library/CloudStorage/Dropbox/1-Documents/00- Dissertation/0-Next_Chapter/Code_and_Data/New SQL and PY Code/Cursor Workspace PDE/520_pipeline_cox_working.ipynb')
nb=json.loads(path.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))

def patch_cell_by_predicate(pred, fn):
    for i,c in enumerate(nb['cells']):
        if c['cell_type']!='markdown':
            continue
        s=''.join(c.get('source',[]))
        if pred(s):
            s2=fn(s)
            if s2==s:
                print('no change for cell',i)
            else:
                nb['cells'][i]['source']=[ln if ln.endswith('\n') else ln+'\n' for ln in s2.splitlines(True)]
                print('patched cell',i)
            return i
    print('cell not found')
    return None

# TOC cell: unique opening
def pred_toc(s):
    return s.startswith('# **🔍 CELL 12: COX REGRESSION MODELS**') and '12.7' in s

def fn_toc(s):
    old="""- **12.6**: Partial effects plots
- **12.7**: Interaction effects (3D plots)
##### **Focus**: Modular Cox regression analysis with progressive saves for troubleshooting
"""
    new="""- **12.6**: Partial effects plots
- **12.6A**: Combined linear + quadratic effects (base + `_sq` pairs)
- **12.7**: Interaction effects (3D surface, contour, slices; `INTERACTION_CONFIG`)
- **12.8**: Model-based promotion curves (Cox `1−S(t)` at fixed low/mid/high X×Y; other covariates at medians)
##### **Focus**: Modular Cox regression analysis with progressive saves for troubleshooting
"""
    if old not in s:
        raise ValueError('TOC pattern not found')
    return s.replace(old,new,1)

patch_cell_by_predicate(pred_toc, fn_toc)

def pred_sum(s):
    return s.startswith('# **✅ PIPELINE SUMMARY**')

def fn_sum(s):
    repls=[
("""   - `df_pipeline_07_time_varying` - Time-varying variables added (includes prestige_unit, prestige_sum if from 502)
""",
"""   - `df_pipeline_07_time_varying` - Time-varying variables added (prestige metrics when enabled — see Cell 7 / `PRESTIGE_CONFIG` in `pipeline_config.py`)
"""),
("""   - `df_pipeline_10_5_cox_zscored` - STandardized + interaction dataset for modeling/plots
""",
"""   - `df_pipeline_10_5_cox_zscored` - Standardized + interaction dataset for modeling/plots (optional; see `CELL10_5S`)
"""),
("""✅ **CELL 11**: Cox analysis and plotting completed
- Enhanced plotting utilities ported from 513 (font scaling, reference lines, subtitles)
- Flexible configuration-based plotting system
- Kaplan-Meier and competing risks plots
- OER filtering support
- Division name plotting support
""",
"""✅ **CELL 11**: Cox analysis and plotting completed
- Enhanced plotting utilities ported from 513 (font scaling, reference lines, subtitles)
- Flexible configuration-based plotting system (`PLOT_CONFIG` in `pipeline_config.py`)
- Kaplan-Meier and competing risks plots; optional CIF bar panels and metadata cards
- Paper-friendly defaults with optional legacy toggles (`show_plot_titles`, `show_plot_subtitles`, legend placement, CIF bar titles/x-labels, etc.)
- OER filtering support
- Division name plotting support
"""),
("""- **12.6**: Partial effects plots (✅ Complete - hazard ratio curves for continuous variables, signal ratios for categorical)
- **12.7**: Interaction effects 3D plots (⏳ Placeholder - ready for expansion)
## **Next Steps**
""",
"""- **12.6**: Partial effects plots (✅ Complete - hazard ratio curves for continuous variables, signal ratios for categorical)
- **12.6A**: Combined linear + quadratic panels (✅ Complete - minimal / full / TB-only / incremental / remove-one views for `base`+`_sq` pairs)
- **12.7**: Interaction effects (✅ Complete - 3D HR surfaces, contour + inset slice, marginal effect plots, distributions/correlation; driven by `INTERACTION_CONFIG`; plot titles respect `PLOT_CONFIG['show_plot_titles']` when that wiring is enabled in the cell)
- **12.8**: Model-based promotion curves (✅ Complete - nine Cox curves at 20th/50th/80th percentiles of `var_x`×`var_y`, other covariates at medians; **uses `var_x`/`var_y` left in memory after 12.7**, typically the last interaction spec)
## **Next Steps**
"""),
("""7. ✅ ~~Implement CELL 12.6 (Partial effects plots)~~ - **COMPLETED**
8. ⏳ **Implement CELL 12.7 (Interaction effects with 3D surface plots)** - Ready for implementation
9. 🔄 **Test pipeline with sample data** - Validate end-to-end workflow
10. 🔄 **Refine filtering parameters in CELL 9** - Optimize based on analysis results
11. 🔄 **Integration testing** - Ensure 502 → 520 pipeline works seamlessly
12. 🔄 **Documentation** - Update overview files with CELL 12 details
""",
"""7. ✅ ~~Implement CELL 12.6 (Partial effects plots)~~ - **COMPLETED**
8. ✅ ~~Implement CELL 12.7 (Interaction effects: 3D surfaces, contours, slices)~~ - **COMPLETED**
9. ✅ ~~Implement CELL 12.8 (Model-based promotion curves at fixed X/Y)~~ - **COMPLETED**
10. 🔄 **Test pipeline with sample data** - Validate end-to-end workflow
11. 🔄 **Refine filtering parameters in CELL 9** - Optimize based on analysis results
12. 🔄 **Integration testing** - Ensure upstream prep → 520 pipeline works seamlessly
13. 🔄 **Documentation** - Keep overview / summary cells aligned with `pipeline_config.py` and new cells
"""),
    ]
    out=s
    for a,b in repls:
        if a not in out:
            raise ValueError('missing pattern:\n'+a[:120])
        out=out.replace(a,b,1)
    return out

patch_cell_by_predicate(pred_sum, fn_sum)

print('saved',path)

Here’s a concise read of **`current_documents/525_plans.md`**:

## What 525 is
- **`525_talent_pool_analysis.ipynb`** (not built yet — **status: planning**): a **parallel track** to 520 that uses 520 outputs to ask **what else** lines up with **strong talent pools** — mainly **who** (senior raters) and **where** (UIC → battalion/brigade, later), as possible **prestige / mechanism** stories beyond OER content.
- Broader paper strategy stays in **`Publication_Plan.md`**.

## Main analysis threads
1. **UIC (rated officer location)** — Company UIC → aggregate to battalion/brigade via **existing UIC tables** (no new hierarchy logic in 525). **Start at division** using **`div_name` from 520 Cell 7**; then add lower levels.
2. **Senior rater (`pid_pde`)** — Who shows up in **strong pools**? Code should be **`rater_role`-style** so adding “rater” later is easy. Optional **`include_lt_rated_oers`** switch.
3. **Longevity & paths** — Tenure from **commissioning / `ofcr_apnt_dt` → exit**; which UICs (and **categorized** types: division, function, etc.) associate with longevity; **sequences** of unit types for **high rank + long tenure**. Caveat: many officers enter the window already CPT/MAJ → **early path may be unobserved** without earlier data.

## Design rules (from the table)
- Senior-rater–first; **520 outputs** preferred; any heavy recompute → **separate `.py`** called from 525, not big inline notebook blocks.

## Proposed notebook outline (§5)
Setup → load 520 + UIC tables → define **“strongest pools”** using **pool mean TB** (not pool-minus-mean) → division-level UIC → senior rater stats → interpretation → extensibility hooks → longevity → categorization → path analysis.

## Important pipeline note (§8)
- **LT OERs on snapshots already exist** when **502 → 512 → 520** with **`load_from_502=True`**: Cell 4 merges OERs to **all** snapshot rows; **LT rows live in 04a–09**; **Cell 10** drops them for Cox. So 525 can **filter for LT rows** from those intermediates — **no new LT OER pipeline** required.

## Open decisions (§6 + bullets at end)
- Exact **520 files/columns** to load; **UIC table names** for battalion/brigade; **pool definition** alignment; where **UIC categorization** lives; **commissioning vs appointment** and **exit** sources; **path** time grain (OER vs year vs assignment).

## Next step in the doc
- Solidify the plan here → create **`525_talent_pool_analysis.ipynb`** with that structure, stubs for loads, then implement division aggregation and senior-rater steps.

If you tell me your immediate goal (e.g. “create the empty notebook + stubs” vs. “lock down which parquet/CSV to load from 520”), we can do that next in this thread.

The codebase already has partial FY alignment at lines 264-269:
- It converts lk[fy_col] to numeric Int64
- But the user's fix was that lookup FY is **two-digit** ('15', '16') and needs **+ 2000** to match snapshot FY (2015, 2016).

The current code does:
```python
lk[fy_col] = pd.to_numeric(lk[fy_col], errors="coerce").astype("Int64")
```

That would give 15, 16, 17 not 2015 - so merge would NOT match df which has 2015.

So I need to:
1. After converting lookup fy to numeric, if values are "century-short" (e.g. all or max FY < 3000 and typical case is 2-digit meaning FY like 15 -> 2015), add 2000 when the value is plausibly a 2-digit fiscal year (e.g. < 100) or use a config flag.

The user's approach: `lk[fy_col] = pd.to_numeric(...).astype("Int64") + 2000` - so they're treating ALL lookup keys as needing +2000. That works if lookup is always '15' style.

But wait - if lookup sometimes has 2015 already, adding 2000 would break (4015). So the robust pattern is:
- If converted value is between 0 and 99 (or 1950-2100 we could detect), add 2000 only when value < 100 (or <= 99)
- Common pattern: two-digit FY as 15 meaning 2015

Let me check build_prestige_uics_by_fy - it groups by "fy" from df_uic_div_lookup - if FY is stored as 15 or 2015 matters for prestige too.

For apply_division_name merge:
```python
lk_fy = pd.to_numeric(lk[fy_col], errors="coerce").astype("Int64")
# 503 JSON keys: two-digit calendar year suffix; map to full year for merge with snapshot fy
mask_short = lk_fy.notna() & (lk_fy < 100)
lk_fy = lk_fy.where(~mask_short, lk_fy + 2000)
lk[fy_col] = lk_fy
```

Actually user said they add 2000 unconditionally: `+ 2000`. That implies their lookup is always 15, 16 not 2015. If some rows are already 2015, +2000 is wrong.

I'll use the conditional: values < 100 (or <= 99) get +2000; values >= 1000 left as-is (already full year). Edge case: FY 99 could mean 1999 not 2099 - military fiscal years in their data seem to be 2015+ from the output.

Alternatively: if max(lk_fy) < 300 and min sensible - use +2000 for all non-null when max < 300? That's fragile.

Safest approach from user: they confirmed their data is '15'..'26' as object. So:
```python
lk_fy = pd.to_numeric(lk[fy_col], errors="coerce").astype("Float64")  # or Int64
# Treat 2-digit / short FY keys from 503 lookup as 2000+year (e.g. 15 -> 2015)
short = lk_fy.notna() & (lk_fy < 100)
lk_fy = np.where(short, lk_fy + 2000, lk_fy)
lk[fy_col] = lk_fy.astype("Int64")
```

Need to import numpy or use pandas:
```python
lk_fy_series = pd.to_numeric(lk[fy_col], errors="coerce")
short_mask = lk_fy_series.notna() & (lk_fy_series < 100)
lk[fy_col] = (lk_fy_series + short_mask * 2000).astype("Int64")  # wrong - can't do that
```
Better:
```python
lk_fy = pd.to_numeric(lk[fy_col], errors="coerce")
lk[fy_col] = (lk_fy + np.where(lk_fy.notna() & (lk_fy < 100), 2000, 0)).astype("Int64")
```
Actually for values >= 100 we add 0; for <100 we need +2000:
```python
lk_fy = pd.to_numeric(lk[fy_col], errors="coerce")
adj = np.where(lk_fy.notna() & (lk_fy < 100), 2000.0, 0.0)
lk[fy_col] = (lk_fy + adj).astype("Int64")
```

For None/nan in div_name: user asks if it's a problem. I'll add a brief comment in code that unmatched UIC×FY left-joins produce NA; plots/filters typically dropna. Optionally normalize pd.NA vs None - after merge pandas usually uses NaN for missing string columns; "None" in unique() might be from literal string None in lookup data - that's data quality not merge.

I'll add optional comment in docstring.

I should also ensure df[fy_col] is normalized the same way if snapshot ever had 15 vs 2015 - the user said snapshot is 2015. The code already does:
```python
fy_vals = pd.to_numeric(df[fy_col], errors="coerce").astype("Int64")
df[fy_col] = fy_vals
```
If snapshot had 15 as int, we'd want +2000 too. For consistency, apply the same short-FY adjustment to **both** sides:

```python
def _normalize_fy_to_full_year(s: pd.Series) -> pd.Series:
    v = pd.to_numeric(s, errors="coerce")
    short = v.notna() & (v < 100)
    v = v.where(~short, v + 2000)
    return v.astype("Int64")
```

Use for both df[fy_col] and lk[fy_col] before merge. That way 15 and 2015 both become 2015... wait 2015 < 100 is false, stays 2015. Good. 15 becomes 2015. Good.

Let me implement _normalize_fy_to_full_year as a private helper at module level and use it in apply_division_name for df[fy_col] and lk[fy_col] after backfill.

Order:
1. df[fy_col] = normalize(df[fy_col])

_**Agent (default)**_

Here’s what landed in the repo and how to think about the nulls.

### Are `None` / `nan` in `div_name` a problem?
Usually **no**. A **left** merge means: no matching **UIC × FY** in the lookup → missing `div_name`. That’s normal (unknown UIC, FY outside the table, typos, etc.). If your plots use **`dropna(subset=['div_name'])`** or a **`filter_null_div`**-style flag, those rows are excluded as intended.

If **`None`** shows up as a **distinct category** next to **`NaN`**, that’s often **real data**: the lookup feather can hold a Python **`None`** / string **`"None"`** for “no division.” That’s different from a merge miss (`NaN`). Worth spot-checking counts; you don’t have to “fix” it unless you want to coerce those to `NaN` for one missing level.

### Cleaner approach than ad‑hoc `+ 2000` everywhere
Your issue was **two representations** of the same FY: **two-digit** (`15` / `"15"`) vs **four-digit** (`2015`), plus **dtype** (`object` vs `Int64`). The durable fix is:

1. **`to_numeric` → nullable `Int64`**
2. **Only for values &lt; 100**, treat them as **20xx** and add **2000** (so **2015–2099** already in the data stay as-is).

That’s what **`_normalize_fy_for_merge`** does. It’s applied to **both** the snapshot **`df[fy_col]`** and the lookup **`lk[fy_col]`**, so you don’t rely on one side always being “full year.” **`backfill_fy`** is also coerced with the same rule if someone ever sets it to `15` instead of `2015`.

**`apply_prestige_unit`** now uses the same normalization on the frame and on each FY key when building the prestige lookup rows, so prestige doesn’t silently break when JSON/feather FYs are two-digit.

### Cursor copy: updated `py_503_hierarchies.py`

New helper + scalar wrapper:

```19:40:py_503_hierarchies.py
def _normalize_fy_for_merge(series: pd.Series) -> pd.Series:
    """
    Coerce fiscal year to nullable Int64 comparable across frames.

    Snapshot data often uses full years (2015, 2016). UIC division lookup from
    503/JSON may use two-digit keys (15, 16) stored as object or int — those
    must become 2015, 2016 before merging. Values already >= 100 are unchanged.
    """
    v = pd.to_numeric(series, errors="coerce")
    short = v.notna() & (v < 100)
    v = v.where(~short, v + 2000)
    return v.astype("Int64")


def _normalize_fy_scalar(fy) -> Optional[int]:
    """Single FY value through the same rules as _normalize_fy_for_merge."""
```

FY + merge path (no debug prints):

```268:289:py_503_hierarchies.py
            df[fy_col] = _normalize_fy_for_merge(df[fy_col])

            if cfg.get("backfill_early_fy", False):
                backfill_fy = cfg.get("backfill_fy")
                if backfill_fy is not None:
                    bf = int(backfill_fy)
                    if bf < 100:
                        bf += 2000
                    df[fy_col] = df[fy_col].where(df[fy_col] >= bf, bf)

            lk = df_uic_lookup[[uic_col, fy_col, div_col]].drop_duplicates().copy()
            lk[fy_col] = _normalize_fy_for_merge(lk[fy_col])

            df = df.merge(lk, on=[uic_col, fy_col], how="left")
            return df
```

You can remove your notebook/debug **`print` / `display`** blocks and keep this module as the single source of truth for FY alignment.


```python
def _expand_two_digit_fy(v: pd.Series | float, pivot: int) -> ...
```

Actually keep logic in one place in _normalize_fy_for_merge:

```python
def _normalize_fy_for_merge(series: pd.Series, two_digit_pivot: int = 68) -> pd.Series:
    v = pd.to_numeric(series, errors="coerce")
    # Full years (e.g. 1995–2100): leave unchanged
    full = v.notna() & (v >= 100)
    short = v.notna() & (v < 100)
    expanded = v.copy()
    # two-digit: yy > pivot → 19yy, else 20yy
    mask_19 = short & (v > two_digit_pivot)
    mask_20 = short & (v <= two_digit_pivot)
    expanded = expanded.where(~mask_19, 1900 + v)
    expanded = expanded.where(~mask_20, 2000 + v)
    # full years untouched (full mask: use where ~short to keep original v for full)
    out = v.where(full, expanded)
    # Wait: for full we want v, for short we want expanded. For na we want na.
    out = pd.Series(index=v.index, dtype="float")
    out = v.copy()
    out = out.where(~mask_19, 1900 + v)
    out = out.where(~mask_20, 2000 + v)
    return out.astype("Int64")
```

Careful: `v.where(~mask_19, 1900+v)` - where mask_19 True use 1900+v, else keep v. Good.
Then `out = out.where(~mask_20, 2000+v)` - but now mask_20 rows might have been updated. For rows that are mask_19, mask_20 is False, second where keeps them. For mask_20 rows, first where kept v (because mask_19 false), second replaces with 2000+v. Good.
For full rows, both masks false, out stays v. Good.
For NaN, short is false (nan < 100 is false in pandas... actually pd.NA < 100 might be NA). Let me check: np.nan < 100 is False. So short is False for nan. out stays nan. Good.

```python
v = pd.to_numeric(series, errors="coerce")
short = v.notna() & (v < 100)
mask_19 = short & (v > two_digit_pivot)
mask_20 = short & (v <= two_digit_pivot)
out = v.astype("float64")
out = out.where(~mask_19, 1900 + v)
out = out.where(~mask_20, 2000 + v)
return out.astype("Int64")
```

For v=1995: short=False, out=1995. Good.
For v=15: mask_20 True, out=2015. Good.
For v=95: mask_19 True, out=1995. Good.

Edge: two_digit_pivot=95? Then 95>95 is false, 95<=95 mask_20, 2095. User can set pivot lower.

`_normalize_fy_scalar`: call with pivot parameter.

Provide concise summary for user about pivot tuning - if they need 20xx for 69-99 they lower pivot to 99 (all yy map to 20yy with pivot high)... Actually if pivot=99, mask_19 = short & (v>99) is always false for v<100, mask_20 = all short, all get 2000+v. Good for "only 20xx 2-digit data"

If they need more 19xx, use pivot 68 default.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Here’s what changed.

### Behavior
- **Values ≥ 100** (e.g. **1995**, **2015**, **2022**) are **unchanged** — so four-digit FYs from the 1990s onward are fine.
- **Values &lt; 100** are treated as **two-digit years** with a **pivot** (default **68**), same idea as a Y2K split:
  - **`yy > pivot`** → **`1900 + yy`** (e.g. **95 → 1995**, **69 → 1969**)
  - **`yy ≤ pivot`** → **`2000 + yy`** (e.g. **15 → 2015**, **68 → 2068**)

So you no longer always do “+2000”, which would have turned **95** into **2095**.

### Config (`pipeline_config.py`)
- **`PRESTIGE_CONFIG['fy_two_digit_pivot']`** = **68**
- **`DIVISION_CONFIG['fy_two_digit_pivot']`** = **68**

Adjust **one or both** if your two-digit convention shifts (e.g. set pivot **99** so **every** two-digit year maps to **20yy** if you never mean 19xx).

### Code (`py_503_hierarchies.py`)
- **`_normalize_fy_for_merge(series, two_digit_pivot=68)`** implements the rules above.
- **`_normalize_fy_scalar`** takes the same pivot.
- **`apply_division_name`** reads **`fy_two_digit_pivot`** from **`DIVISION_CONFIG`**; **`backfill_fy`** is normalized with the same pivot (no special-case `+2000` only).
- **`apply_prestige_unit`** accepts optional **`prestige_config`** and uses **`fy_two_digit_pivot`** from **`PRESTIGE_CONFIG`**; **`ensure_prestige_metrics`** passes **`cfg`** through.

### Optional argument
- **`apply_prestige_unit(..., prestige_config=None)`** — existing callers without that argument still work; **`None`** uses **`PRESTIGE_CONFIG`**.

---

The user believes these are created in Cell 10.5 instead. I need to trace the pipeline in the notebook/code to explain the order of operations:

1. Cell 10 builds Cox survival intervals - it likely checks that all columns needed for the model exist in the data BEFORE or AT the point of building intervals from snapshot data
2. Cell 10.5 might add z-scored columns and interactions

If Cell 10 runs first and expects those columns to exist in the snapshot-derived frame for time-varying covariates during interval construction, then creating them in 10.5 would be TOO LATE - the intervals might already be built without those columns, or the warning is a pre-flight check.

```
                end_times[:-1] = (officer_snapshots['snpsht_dt'].iloc[1:] - officer_info['dor_cpt']).dt.days
            
            # For last snapshot: determine based on competing event
            # Ensure dor_cpt is a scalar datetime for calculations
            dor_cpt_dt = pd.to_datetime(officer_info['dor_cpt'])
            
            if pd.notna(officer_info['dor_maj']):
                # Promoted - end at promotion date
                dor_maj_dt = pd.to_datetime(officer_info['dor_maj'])
                end_times[-1] = (dor_maj_dt - dor_cpt_dt).days
                events = np.zeros(n_snapshots)
                events[-1] = 1  # Promoted
            elif officer_info['last_snapshot'] &lt; study_end_date:
                # Attrited - end at last snapshot
                last_snapshot_dt = pd.to_datetime(officer_info['last_snapshot'])
                end_times[-1] = (last_snapshot_dt - dor_cpt_dt).days
                events = np.zeros(n_snapshots)
                events[-1] = 2  # Attrited
            else:
                # Censored - end at study end
                study_end_dt = pd.to_datetime(study_end_date)
                end_times[-1] = (study_end_dt - dor_cpt_dt).days
                events = np.zeros(n_snapshots)
                events[-1] = 0  # Censored
            
            # DIAGNOSTIC: Check what event ws assigned (first 5 officers only)
            # Create survival records with time-varying covariates
            survival_dict = {
                'pid_pde': pid,
                'start_time': start_times,
                'stop_time': end_times,
                'event': events,
                # Static covariates (repeated for each interval)
                'sex': officer_info['sex'],
                'yg': officer_info['yg'],
                # Time-varying covariates from snapshots
                'snpsht_dt': officer_snapshots['snpsht_dt'],
            }
            
            # Add optional static columns if they exist
            if 'age_cpt' in officer_info.index and pd.notna(officer_info.get('age_cpt')):
                survival_dict['age_cpt'] = officer_info['age_cpt']
            if 'final_job_code' in officer_info.index and pd.notna(officer_info.get('final_job_code')):
                survival_dict['final_job_code'] = officer_info['final_job_code']
            
            # Add time-varying OER columns
            for col in available_oer_cols:
                if col in officer_snapshots.columns:
                    survival_dict[col] = officer_snapshots[col].values
            
            # Add other time-varying columns if they exist
            string_fill_cols = {'div_name', 'asg_uic_pde', 'rank_pde', 'job_code'}
```

_**Agent (default)**_

Yes — your reading is right.

**What Cell 10 is doing:** It loads `df_pipeline_09_filtered`, sorts it as `df_snapshots_sorted`, then builds survival intervals. The loop only pulls time-varying values for columns that are actually on that frame (`available_oer_cols` and `available_other_tv_cols`).  

**What the message is doing:** It compares **all** of `COLUMN_CONFIG['model_time_varying_cols']` (including `z_*`, `*_sq`, `star_pool_interaction`) to **that same snapshot table**. Those names are **not** supposed to be on `09_filtered`; they are created later in **Cell 10.5** on the Cox-ready output (`df_pipeline_10_cox_ready` → `df_pipeline_10_5_cox_zscored`). So the warning is **diagnostic**, not “Cell 10 is broken.”  

**`div_name` showing as present:** That one *can* be on snapshots (e.g. from Cell 7), so it correctly appears under “model-only time-varying (in snapshot data).”

The old follow-up line (“fix it in Cell 8 or 9”) was misleading for z-scored/quadratic/interaction terms. I updated **Cell 10** in `520_pipeline_cox_working.ipynb` so the second line explains that **missing `z_*` / `*_sq` / interactions is often expected before 10.5**, and reminds you to run **Cell 10.5** and use the z-scored frame in Cell 12 when the model is set up that way.

```json
            "text": "        # Each plot is a dictionary with required keys:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 972,
            "text": "        #   - 'name': str (unique identifier, used internally and in metadata)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 973,
            "text": "        #              NOTE: Actual filename is auto-generated with enhanced description"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 974,
            "text": "        #   - 'variable': str or None (column name to analyze, must be in COLUMN_CONFIG, or None for grouping only)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 975,
            "text": "        #   - 'group_by': str or None (column name to group by, creates separate curves, or None)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 976,
            "text": "        #   - 'plot_type': str ('kaplan_meier' or 'competing_risks')"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 977,
            "text": "        #   - 'bin_continuous': bool (True for continuous variables, False for categorical)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 978,
            "text": "        #   - 'n_bins': int (number of bins, ignored if bin_continuous=False)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 979,
            "text": "        #   - 'bin_method': str ('quantile' or 'equal_width', ignored if bin_continuous=False)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 980,
            "text": "        #   - 'min_group_size': int (minimum officers per group)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 981,
            "text": "        #   - 'use_time_varying': bool (True for time-varying vars, False for static, default True)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 982,
            "text": "        #   - 'filter_zero_oer': bool (True to exclude officers with no OER data, only for OER variables)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 983,
            "text": "        #   - 'filter_nan_variable': bool (True to drop rows where variable is NaN)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 984,
            "text": "        #   - 'filter_nan_group_by': bool (True to drop rows where group_by is NaN)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 985,
            "text": "        #   - 'strict_equal_n': bool (force equal-N bins with rank-based qcut)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 986,
            "text": "        #   - 'x_max_days': int or None (cap x-axis at N days)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 987,
            "text": "        #   - 'bin_group_by': bool (True to bin group_by separately)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 988,
            "text": "        #   - 'group_bins': int (number of bins for group_by, defaults to n_bins)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 989,
            "text": "        #   - 'group_bin_method': str ('quantile' or 'equal_width' for group_by)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 990,
            "text": "        #"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 991,
            "text": "        # OUTPUT FILES:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 992,
            "text": "        #   - Plot: {plot_type}_{variable}_{group_by}_{bins}_{method}_{filter}_{dates}.png"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 993,
            "text": "        #   - Metadata: {plot_type}_{variable}_{group_by}_{bins}_{method}_{filter}_{dates}_metadata.json"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 994,
            "text": "        ########################################################################################################################################"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 995,
            "text": "        {    # 1: TB ratio (mode-specific, raw)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 996,
            "text": "            'variable': TB_RATIO_SNR_COL,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 997,
            "text": "            'name': 'cr_' + TB_RATIO_SNR_COL,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 998,
            "text": "            'group_by': None,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 999,
            "text": "            'plot_type': 'competing_risks',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1000,
            "text": "            'bin_continuous': True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1001,
            "text": "            'n_bins': 5,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1002,
            "text": "            'bin_method': 'quantile',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1003,
            "text": "            'min_group_size': 3,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1004,
            "text": "            'use_time_varying': True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1005,
            "text": "            'filter_zero_oer': True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1006,
            "text": "            'filter_nan_variable': True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1007,
            "text": "            'filter_nan_group_by': True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1008,
            "text": "            'strict_equal_n': False,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1009,
            "text": "            'x_min_days': 1000,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1010,
            "text": "            'x_max_days': 4500,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1011,
            "text": "            'bin_group_by': True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1012,
            "text": "            'group_bins': 3,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1013,
            "text": "            'group_bin_method': 'equal_width',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1014,
            "text": "        },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1015,
            "text": "########################################################################################################################################"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1016,
            "text": "        {    # 2: Pool minus-mean (raw)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1017,
            "text": "            'variable': POOL_MINUS_MEAN_SNR_COL"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 47,
            "line": 1016
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 94,
            "line": 967
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./pipeline_config.py",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.4514646530151367
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 827,
            "text": "def plot_competing_risks_cif_bars("
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 828,
            "text": "    plot_df,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 829,
            "text": "    plot_spec,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 830,
            "text": "    metadata,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 831,
            "text": "    output_dir=None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 832,
            "text": "    filename_base=None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 833,
            "text": "    dpi=300,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 834,
            "text": "    figsize=(15, 10),"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 835,
            "text": "    legend_outside=True,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 836,
            "text": "):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 837,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 838,
            "text": "    Plot bar charts of final CIF by bin for promotion and attrition."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 839
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 840,
            "text": "    Bars are ordered by the mean of the plotted variable (ascending),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 841,
            "text": "    which yields z-score ascending order when plotting z-scored variables."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 842,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 843,
            "text": "    if plot_df is None or plot_df.empty:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 844,
            "text": "        print(\"⚠️ No data for CIF bar plots.\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 845,
            "text": "        return None"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 846
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 847,
            "text": "    var_name = plot_spec.get(\"variable\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 848,
            "text": "    group_col = \"plot_group\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 849,
            "text": "    if group_col not in plot_df.columns:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 850,
            "text": "        print(\"⚠️ plot_group missing; cannot build CIF bars.\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 851,
            "text": "        return None"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 852
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 853,
            "text": "    # Compute per-group stats"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 854,
            "text": "    group_stats = []"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 855,
            "text": "    for group, gdf in plot_df.groupby(group_col):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 856,
            "text": "        # Match competing-risks filtering: require valid stop time"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 857,
            "text": "        if \"stop_time\" in gdf.columns:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 858,
            "text": "            gdf = gdf[gdf[\"stop_time\"].notna() \u0026 (gdf[\"stop_time\"] \u003e 0)].copy()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 859,
            "text": "        n = len(gdf)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 860,
            "text": "        if n == 0:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 861,
            "text": "            continue"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 862,
            "text": "        promo_rate = (gdf[\"event\"] == 1).sum() / n if \"event\" in gdf.columns else np.nan"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 863,
            "text": "        attr_rate = (gdf[\"event\"] == 2).sum() / n if \"event\" in gdf.columns else np.nan"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 864,
            "text": "        if var_name and var_name in gdf.columns:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 865,
            "text": "            mean_val = pd.to_numeric(gdf[var_name], errors=\"coerce\").mean()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 866,
            "text": "            min_val = pd.to_numeric(gdf[var_name], errors=\"coerce\").min()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 867,
            "text": "            max_val = pd.to_numeric(gdf[var_name], errors=\"coerce\").max()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 868,
            "text": "        else:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 869,
            "text": "            mean_val = min_val = max_val = np.nan"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 870,
            "text": "        group_stats.append({"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 871,
            "text": "            \"group\": group,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 872,
            "text": "            \"n\": n,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 873,
            "text": "            \"promo_rate\": promo_rate,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 874,
            "text": "            \"attr_rate\": attr_rate,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 875,
            "text": "            \"mean_val\": mean_val,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 876,
            "text": "            \"min_val\": min_val,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 877,
            "text": "            \"max_val\": max_val,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 878,
            "text": "        })"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 879
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 880,
            "text": "    if not group_stats:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 881,
            "text": "        print(\"⚠️ No group stats for CIF bar plots.\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 882,
            "text": "        return None"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 883
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 884,
            "text": "    # Sort by mean of variable (ascending). Fallback to group label order."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 1012
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 22,
            "line": 823
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./zips/TALENT_NET_export_20260305-0835/cox_plot_helpers.py",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.4422611892223358
    },

          },
          {

          {
            "lineNumber": 3878,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3879,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3880,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3881,
            "text": "        \"                    print_v(f\\\"  ✅ Created CIF bar plot: {filename_base.replace('.png','')}_cif_bars.png\\\")\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3882,

          {

          {
            "lineNumber": 3884,

      "codeBlock": {

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "from pipeline_config import *"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "# RUN 1: Individual TB ratio vs pool minus mean (one place for scale + variables)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "# Set RUN_SCALE to 'raw' or 'z' to control model, CR plots, PE plots, and interaction plots."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "RUN_SCALE = 'z'"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "# One-swipe defaults for all CR plots (used by automated plots and by individual plots when USE_OVERRIDE_CR_PLOTS is True)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "RUN_CR_PLOT_DEFAULTS = {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "    'group_by': None,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "    'plot_type': 'competing_risks',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "    'bin_continuous': True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "    'n_bins': 8,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "    'cif_bar_hspace': 0.1,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "    # Key name must match cox_plot_helpers.plot_competing_risks_cif_bars (cif_bar_show_legend)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "    'cif_bar_show_legend': False,  # set True to show legend on CIF bar plots (read in cox_plot_helpers)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "    'bin_method': 'equal_width',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "    'min_group_size': 3,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "    'use_time_varying': True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "    'filter_zero_oer': True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "    'filter_nan_variable': True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "    'filter_nan_group_by': True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "    'strict_equal_n': False,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "    'x_min_days': 0,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "    'x_max_days': 4500,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "    'bin_group_by': True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "    'group_bins': 3,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "    'group_bin_method': 'equal_width',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "# Quadratics: set in one place here via quadratic_bases. Order of operations (in Cell 10.5): raw → square → then z-score the square if RUN_SCALE is 'z'."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 156,
            "line": 30
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./pipeline_config_17_1.py",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.4217151999473572
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 832,
            "text": "def plot_competing_risks_cif_bars("
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 833,
            "text": "    plot_df,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 834,
            "text": "    plot_spec,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 835,
            "text": "    metadata,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 836,
            "text": "    output_dir=None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 837,
            "text": "    filename_base=None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 838,
            "text": "    dpi=300,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 839,
            "text": "    figsize=(10, 9),"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 840,
            "text": "    tick_sz=None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 841,
            "text": "    legend_outside=True,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 842,
            "text": "):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 843,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 844,
            "text": "    Plot bar charts of final CIF by bin for promotion and attrition."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 845
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 846,
            "text": "    Bars are ordered by the mean of the plotted variable (ascending),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 847,
            "text": "    which yields z-score ascending order when plotting z-scored variables."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 848,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 849,
            "text": "    if plot_df is None or plot_df.empty:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 850,
            "text": "        print(\"⚠️ No data for CIF bar plots.\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 851,
            "text": "        return None"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 852
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 853,
            "text": "    var_name = plot_spec.get(\"variable\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 854,
            "text": "    group_col = \"plot_group\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 855,
            "text": "    if group_col not in plot_df.columns:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 856,
            "text": "        print(\"⚠️ plot_group missing; cannot build CIF bars.\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 857,
            "text": "        return None"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 858
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 859,
            "text": "    # Compute per-group stats"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 860,
            "text": "    group_stats = []"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 861,
            "text": "    for group, gdf in plot_df.groupby(group_col):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 862,
            "text": "        # Match competing-risks filtering: require valid stop time"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 863,
            "text": "        if \"stop_time\" in gdf.columns:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 864,
            "text": "            gdf = gdf[gdf[\"stop_time\"].notna() \u0026 (gdf[\"stop_time\"] \u003e 0)].copy()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 865,
            "text": "        n = len(gdf)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 866,
            "text": "        if n == 0:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 867,
            "text": "            continue"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 868,
            "text": "        promo_rate = (gdf[\"event\"] == 1).sum() / n if \"event\" in gdf.columns else np.nan"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 869,
            "text": "        attr_rate = (gdf[\"event\"] == 2).sum() / n if \"event\" in gdf.columns else np.nan"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 870,
            "text": "        if var_name and var_name in gdf.columns:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 871,
            "text": "            mean_val = pd.to_numeric(gdf[var_name], errors=\"coerce\").mean()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 872,
            "text": "            min_val = pd.to_numeric(gdf[var_name], errors=\"coerce\").min()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 873,
            "text": "            max_val = pd.to_numeric(gdf[var_name], errors=\"coerce\").max()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 874,
            "text": "        else:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 875,
            "text": "            mean_val = min_val = max_val = np.nan"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 876,
            "text": "        group_stats.append({"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 877,
            "text": "            \"group\": group,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 878,
            "text": "            \"n\": n,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 879,
            "text": "            \"promo_rate\": promo_rate,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 880,
            "text": "            \"attr_rate\": attr_rate,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 881,
            "text": "            \"mean_val\": mean_val,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 882,
            "text": "            \"min_val\": min_val,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 883,
            "text": "            \"max_val\": max_val,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 884,
            "text": "        })"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 885
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 886,
            "text": "    if not group_stats:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 887,
            "text": "        print(\"⚠️ No group stats for CIF bar plots.\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 888,
            "text": "        return None"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 889
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 890,
            "text": "    # Sort by mean of variable (ascending). Fallback to group label order."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 1070
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 22,
            "line": 828
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./obsolete_files/cox_plot_helpers_20Mar.py",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.41953974962234497
    },
```
