cell 51 len 66590
def prepare_plot_data(df, plot_spec):
            """
            Prepare data for plotting based on plot specification
            Handles time-varying survival interval data
            
            For Kaplan-Meier plots: Aggregates to officer-level FIRST, then creates groups
            This ensures each officer appears in exactly one group based on their final characteristics
            
            Returns:
            --------
            DataFrame ready for plotting, or None if insufficient data
            """
            plot_df = df.copy()
            
            # For Kaplan-Meier plots, we MUST aggregate to officer-level first
            # Otherwise, officers can appear in multiple groups as their cumulative metrics change
            # For competing risks, we also aggregate to officer-level for KM fitting
            
            # Always aggregate to officer-level for plotting (KM requires one row per officer)
            # Use last interval for each officer (contains final event and stop_time)
            officer_df = plot_df.sort_values(['pid_pde', 'stop_time']).groupby('pid_pde').last().reset_index()
            
            # Now work with officer-level data
            working_df = officer_df.copy()
            # print(f"\n\n\n WORKING_DF.COLUMNS:  {working_df.columns}") #####################  DEBUG ######################
            # DEBUG: time alignment for backward evals (uses last snapshot per officer)
            # Optional debug: print_v(f"Variable: {plot_spec.get('variable')}")
            if plot_spec.get('variable') and '_bwd' in str(plot_spec.get('variable')):
                if 'eval_thru_dt_bwd' in working_df.columns and 'snpsht_dt' in working_df.columns:
                    bwd_missing = working_df['eval_thru_dt_bwd'].isna().mean()
                    bwd_future = (working_df['eval_thru_dt_bwd'] &gt; working_df['snpsht_dt']).mean()
                    print_v(f"  • DEBUG bwd time alignment: missing eval_thru_dt_bwd={bwd_missing:.1%}, eval_thru_dt_bwd after snpsht_dt={bwd_future:.1%}")
                else:
                    print_v("  ⚠️ DEBUG skipped bwd time alignment check (required columns missing)")
                
                
            # Filter out officers with NO OER data if requested and variable is OER-related
            # NOTE: We only filter officers who have NO OER data at all (NaN), not those with OER data but 0 top blocks
            if plot_spec.get('filter_zero_oer', False) and plot_spec['variable'] is not None:
                var_col = plot_spec['variable']
                if var_col in working_df.columns:
                    # Check if variable is OER-related using manual list from COLUMN_CONFIG
                    oer_variables = COLUMN_CONFIG.get('oer_variables', [])
                    is_oer_variable = var_col in oer_variables
                    
                    if is_oer_variable:
                        before_count = len(working_df)
                        
                        # Check if officer has ANY OER data by looking at OER variables from config
                        # If they have OER data, at least one of these should be non-NaN
                        # Find which OER columns exist in the data
                        available_oer_cols = [col for col in oer_variables if col in working_df.columns]
                        
                        if len(available_oer_cols) &gt; 0:
                            # Officer has OER data if ANY OER column is non-NaN
                            # (even if the specific variable we're plotting is NaN)
                            has_oer_data = working_df[available_oer_cols].notna().any(axis=1)
                            working_df = working_df[has_oer_data].copy()
                        else:
                            # Fallback: just check the specific variable (if no other OER columns available)
                            # Filter out officers where variable is NaN (no OER data for this specific metric)
                            working_df = working_df[working_df[var_col].notna()].copy()
                        
                        after_count = len(working_df)
                        removed = before_count - after_count
                        if removed &gt; 0:
                            print_v(f"  • Filtered {removed:,} officers with NO OER data ({removed/before_count*100:.1f}%)")
                            print_v(f"  • Remaining officers: {after_count:,} (includes officers with 0 top blocks but with OER data)")
            
            # Optionally drop rows where the plotted variable is NaN
            if plot_spec.get('filter_nan_variable', False) and plot_spec['variable'] is not None:
                var_col = plot_spec['variable']
                if var_col in working_df.columns:
                    before_count = len(working_df)
                    working_df = working_df[working_df[var_col].notna()].copy()
                    removed = before_count - len(working_df)
                    if removed &gt; 0:
                        print_v(
                            f"  • Filtered {removed:,} rows with NaN '{var_col}' "
                            f"({removed/before_count*100:.1f}%)"
                        )
                        print_v(f"  • Remaining officers: {len(working_df):,}")
            
            # DEBUG: report slice used for binning
            print_v(f"  • DEBUG slice for {plot_spec['variable']}: rows={len(working_df):,}")
            if plot_spec['variable'] in working_df.columns:
                print_v(f"  non-null rate: {working_df[plot_spec['variable']].notna().mean():.3f}")
            
            # Optionally drop rows where the group_by variable is NaN
            if plot_spec.get('filter_nan_group_by', False) and plot_spec['group_by'] is not None:
                group_col = plot_spec['group_by']
                if group_col in working_df.columns:
                    before_count = len(working_df)
                    working_df = working_df[working_df[group_col].notna()].copy()
                    removed = before_count - len(working_df)
                    if removed &gt; 0:
                        print_v(
                            f"  • Filtered {removed:,} rows with NaN group_by '{group_col}' "
                            f"({removed/before_count*100:.1f}%)"
                        )
                        print_v(f"  • Remaining officers: {len(working_df):,}")
                        
            # Handle variable binning if needed
            if plot_spec['variable'] is not None:
                if plot_spec['variable'] not in working_df.columns:
                    print_v(f"⚠️ Variable '{plot_spec['variable']}' not found in columns: {list(working_df.columns)[:10]}...")
                    print_v(f"⚠️ Skipping plot: {plot_spec['name']}")
                    return None
                
                if plot_spec['bin_continuous']:
                    var_col = plot_spec['variable']
                    if working_df[var_col].dtype in ['float64', 'int64']:
                        # Check if it's actually continuous (more than 10 unique values)
                        if working_df[var_col].nunique() &gt; 10:
                            working_df[f"{var_col}_binned"] = create_bins(
                                working_df, var_col, 
                                n_bins=plot_spec['n_bins'],
                                method=plot_spec['bin_method'],
                                strict_equal_n=plot_spec.get('strict_equal_n', False)
                            )
                            grouping_col = f"{var_col}_binned"
    
                            # DIAGNOSTIC: print_bin_ranges
                            print_v(f"\n📊 Bin ranges for {var_col}:")
                            for bin_label in working_df[grouping_col].cat.categories if hasattr(working_df[grouping_col], 'cat') else working_df[grouping_col].unique():
                                if pd.notna(bin_label):
                                    bin_data = working_df[working_df[grouping_col] == bin_label][var_col]
                                    print_v(f"  • {bin_label}: range [{bin_data.min():.2f} - {bin_data.max():.2f}], mean={bin_data.mean():.2f}, n={len(bin_data):,}")
                        else:
                            grouping_col = var_col
                    else:
                        grouping_col = var_col
                else:
                    grouping_col = plot_spec['variable']
            else:
                grouping_col = None
            
            # Use group_by column (if specified)
            group_col = plot_spec.get('group_by', None)
            
            if group_col is not None:
                if group_col not in working_df.columns:
                    print_v(f"⚠️ Grouping column '{group_col}' not found in columns: {list(working_df.columns)[:10]}...")
                    print_v(f"⚠️ Skipping plot: {plot_spec['name']}")

            if group_col is not None:

                    print_v(f"⚠️ Skipping plot: {plot_spec['name']}")
                    return None
            
            # Optional: bin the group_by column separately (for 3x3, 4x2, etc.)
            group_col_for_plot = group_col
            if group_col is not None and plot_spec.get('bin_group_by', False):
                group_bins = plot_spec.get('group_bins', plot_spec.get('n_bins', 3))
                group_bin_method = plot_spec.get('group_bin_method', plot_spec.get('bin_method', 'quantile'))
                if group_col in working_df.columns:
                    if working_df[group_col].dtype in ['float64', 'int64'] and working_df[group_col].nunique() &gt; 10:
                        working_df[f"{group_col}_binned"] = create_bins(
                            working_df, group_col,
                            n_bins=group_bins,
                            method=group_bin_method,
                            strict_equal_n=plot_spec.get('strict_equal_n', False)
                        )
                        group_col_for_plot = f"{group_col}_binned"
                        # DIAGNOSTIC: print_bin_ranges for group_by
                        print_v(f"\n📊 Group-by bin ranges for {group_col}:")
                        for bin_label in working_df[group_col_for_plot].cat.categories if hasattr(working_df[group_col_for_plot], 'cat') else working_df[group_col_for_plot].unique():
                            if pd.notna(bin_label):
                                bin_data = working_df[working_df[group_col_for_plot] == bin_label][group_col]
                                print_v(f"  • {bin_label}: range [{bin_data.min():.2f} - {bin_data.max():.2f}], mean={bin_data.mean():.2f}, n={len(bin_data):,}")
                    else:
                        group_col_for_plot = group_col
            
            # Create final grouping column
            if grouping_col is not None and group_col_for_plot is not None:
                # Combine variable and group_by for multi-level grouping
                working_df['plot_group'] = (working_df[group_col_for_plot].astype(str) + " | " + 
                                           working_df[grouping_col].astype(str))
            elif group_col_for_plot is not None:
                # Just use group_by column (possibly binned)
                working_df['plot_group'] = working_df[group_col_for_plot].astype(str)
            elif grouping_col is not None:
                # Just use the binned variable (no group_by)
                working_df['plot_group'] = working_df[grouping_col].astype(str)
            else:
                # Fallback: use a default group
                working_df['plot_group'] = 'All'
            
            # Filter to minimum group size (count rows since we're at officer-level)
            group_counts = working_df['plot_group'].value_counts()
            
            print_v(f"  • Group counts (before filtering): {dict(group_counts.sort_values(ascending=False))}")
            
            valid_groups = group_counts[group_counts &gt;= plot_spec['min_group_size']].index
            working_df = working_df[working_df['plot_group'].isin(valid_groups)].copy()
            
            if len(working_df) == 0:
                print_v(f"⚠️ No groups meet minimum size requirement ({plot_spec['min_group_size']} officers)")
                print_v(f"⚠️ Largest group had {group_counts.max() if len(group_counts) &gt; 0 else 0} officers")
                print_v(f"⚠️ Skipping plot: {plot_spec['name']}")
                return None
            
            print_v(f"  • Valid groups after filtering: {len(valid_groups)} groups with &gt;= {plot_spec['min_group_size']} officers each")
            return working_df

Display: variable_label | group_by_label = Bin 1 | 75RR. That matches the user's legend "Bin 1 ... | 75RR".

For cleaner plot without fake bin:

def plot_competing_risks(df, plot_spec, save_path, metadata):
            """Create competing risks plot using simple proportion CIF calculation"""
            fig, (ax1, ax2) = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(PLOT_CONFIG['figsize'][0], PLOT_CONFIG['figsize'][1]*1.5))
            
            # Get unique groups and sort by average of binned variable (if avaialable)
            # This ensures that legend shows bins from highest to lowest average value
            groups = df['plot_group'].unique()
            
            # Get variable name for legend enhancement
            var_name = plot_spec.get('variable')

            def _hex_to_rgb(hex_color):
                hex_color = hex_color.lstrip('#')
                return tuple(int(hex_color[i:i+2], 16) / 255.0 for i in (0, 2, 4))

            def _rgb_to_hex(rgb):
                return "#%02x%02x%02x" % tuple(int(round(c * 255)) for c in rgb)

            def _build_hex_gradient(hex_colors, n_colors):
                if not hex_colors:
                    return None
                if len(hex_colors) == n_colors:
                    return hex_colors
                if len(hex_colors) == 1:
                    return [hex_colors[0]] * n_colors
                start = _hex_to_rgb(hex_colors[0])
                end = _hex_to_rgb(hex_colors[-1])
                gradient = []
                for i in range(n_colors):
                    t = i / max(n_colors - 1, 1)
                    rgb = (
                        start[0] + (end[0] - start[0]) * t,
                        start[1] + (end[1] - start[1]) * t,
                        start[2] + (end[2] - start[2]) * t,
                    )
                    gradient.append(_rgb_to_hex(rgb))
                return gradient
            
            # If a variable is being binned, sort groups by its average value
            ordered_groups = list(groups)
            if var_name is not None and var_name in df.columns:
                # Calculate average of variable for each group
                group_means = df.groupby('plot_group')[var_name].mean()
                # Sort groups by average value (highest to lowest)
                groups = sorted(groups, key=lambda g: group_means.get(g, float('-inf')), reverse=True)
                # Ascending order for palette mapping (Q1..Qn)
                ordered_groups = sorted(group_means.index, key=lambda g: group_means.get(g, float('inf')))
                print_v(f"  • Sorted groups by average {var_name} (highest to lowest)")

            # Always use distinct palettes for promo vs attr (blue vs orange)
            promo_palette = plot_spec.get('cif_bar_palette_promo') or ["#d7e6f5", "#1f5aa6"]
            attr_palette = plot_spec.get('cif_bar_palette_attr') or ["#fde2cf", "#c0581a"]
            
            promo_palette = _build_hex_gradient(promo_palette, len(ordered_groups))
            attr_palette = _build_hex_gradient(attr_palette, len(ordered_groups))
            
            promo_color_map = {g: promo_palette[i] for i, g in enumerate(ordered_groups)}
            attr_color_map = {g: attr_palette[i] for i, g in enumerate(ordered_groups)}
            
            promo_colors = [promo_color_map.get(g, '#4c4c4c') for g in groups]
            attr_colors = [attr_color_map.get(g, '#4c4c4c') for g in groups]
            # print("# Plot 1: Promotion events (event == 1)******************************************************")
            # Plot 1: Promotion events (event == 1)
            for i, group in enumerate(groups):
                group_data = df[df['plot_group'] == group].copy()
                
                if len(group_data) == 0:
                    continue
                # print("# Filter out invalid data)******************************************************")
                # Filter out invalid data
                valid_mask = (group_data['stop_time'].notna()) &amp; (group_data['stop_time'] &gt; 0)
                group_data = group_data[valid_mask].copy()
                
                if len(group_data) == 0:
                    print_v(f"  ⚠️ No valid officers in group '{group}' after filtering")
                    continue
                # print("# Calculate CIF for promotion using simple proportion approach)******************************************************")
                # Calculate CIF for promotion using simple proportion approach
                times, promotion_cif = calculate_cif(group_data, event_type=1)
                # print("# Debug:

```

```python
def _discrete_cycle_cmap(name, n):
    cmap = plt.cm.get_cmap(name)
    return [mcolors.to_hex(cmap(i / (cmap.N - 1))[:3]) for i in [j % cmap.N for j in range(n)]]
```

_**Agent (default)**_

Here are direct answers and what was implemented.

### 1. Straighter way than “one bin”
Yes. The one-bin trick was only there because **`plot_group` was built as `div_name | variable_bin`**, so every curve had to include a bin label.

**Config-only approach (now supported):**

- Keep **`variable`** set to your TB column so **`filter_nan_variable`** / OER filters still apply.
- Set **`group_only_curves: True`** so **`plot_group` is only `div_name`** (no bin segment, no `"Bin 1 | …"` in the legend).
- For this plot, set **`bin_continuous: False`** so the pipeline does not run **`create_bins`** at all (faster, and filenames use the categorical pattern instead of `b8_…`).

`pipeline_config_div_name.py` is updated so **`run1_cr_tb_by_div`** uses that pattern.

**Alternative:** **`variable: None`** and **`group_by: 'div_name'`** also gives one curve per division, but you lose **`filter_nan_variable`** on the TB column unless you add separate logic later.

---

### 2. Old (non-monochrome) colors for this plot only
Competing-risks code was using **blue / orange hex gradients** (`cif_bar_palette_promo` / `_attr`). Legacy KM used **discrete `Set1`**.

**New optional flag on the plot spec:** **`cr_color_mode: 'set1'`**  
That builds a **discrete list from Set1 → Set2 → Set3** (cycling if there are many divisions) and uses the **same color for a division on both** promotion and attrition panels so you can match lines across plots.

Your **`run1_cr_tb_by_div`** entry sets **`cr_color_mode: 'set1'`**. For other plots, omit it (or set to anything other than `'set1'`) to keep the blue/orange gradients.

Implementation is in **`520_pipeline_cox_working.ipynb`** Cell 11 (`plot_competing_risks`).

---

### 3. **`Unknown` dominating `div_name`**
Understood — no code change. When you revisit it, the FY/UIC merge path you fixed in **`py_503_hierarchies`** is the right place to look; **`Unknown`** is usually “no UIC×FY match in the lookup,” not a plotting bug.

---

**Files touched:** `520_pipeline_cox_working.ipynb` (cell 51: `group_only_curves` + `cr_color_mode`), `pipeline_config_div_name.py` (TB-by-division plot spec).

---

_**User**_

Cell 11 output.  I'm getting a whole bunch of groups for some reason with only 15 unique div_name values

                    if plot_spec.get('group_only_curves') or nu > 20:
                        if nu > 20 and not plot_spec.get('group_only_curves'):
                            print_v(
                                f"  • Auto: omit `{gn}` from plot_group (nunique={nu}) with bin_continuous=False and group_by={group_col_for_plot!r}; "
                                "curves are one per group. Set bin_continuous=True to bin the variable."
                            )
                        grouping_col = None
```

_**Agent (default)**_

Identifying the issue: with `bin_continuous=False`, `grouping_col` is set to the variable column name, so `plot_group` becomes `div_name + " | " + str(z_value)` — one curve per unique (division, float), causing ~319 groups. `group_only_curves` should clear that but may be missing from the active config or not applied. Implementing a unified safeguard: when `group_by` is set, the variable is numeric, `bin_continuous` is False, and nunique > 20 (or `group_only_curves`), omit the variable from `plot_group`.

import json
from pathlib import Path
path = Path('/Users/charleslevine/Library/CloudStorage/Dropbox/1-Documents/00- Dissertation/0-Next_Chapter/Code_and_Data/New SQL and PY Code/Cursor Workspace PDE/520_pipeline_cox_working.ipynb')
nb = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))
for i, c in enumerate(nb['cells']):
    if c['cell_type'] != 'code':
        continue
    s = ''.join(c.get('source', []))
    if 'group_only_curves' not in s or 'def prepare_plot_data' not in s:
        continue
    old = """            # Stratify CIF/KM curves by group_by only: keep `variable` for filters/OER logic but omit
            # variable bins from plot_group and legend (e.g. div_name curves without "Bin 1 | ...").
            if plot_spec.get('group_only_curves') and group_col is not None:
                grouping_col = None
            
            # Create final grouping column"""
    new = """            # plot_group must not be group_by | <each distinct float> when variable is continuous and
            # bin_continuous=False (that creates hundreds of spurious curves). Omit variable from the
            # composite when group_only_curves is True, or auto when numeric nunique is large.
            if group_col_for_plot is not None and grouping_col is not None:
                gn = grouping_col
                if gn in working_df.columns and pd.api.types.is_numeric_dtype(working_df[gn]) and not plot_spec.get('bin_continuous', False):
                    nu = int(working_df[gn].nunique())
                    if plot_spec.get('group_only_curves') or nu > 20:
                        if nu > 20 and not plot_spec.get('group_only_curves'):
                            print_v(
                                f"  • Auto: omit `{gn}` from plot_group (nunique={nu}) with bin_continuous=False and "
                                f"group_by={group_col_for_plot!r} — one curve per group. "
                                f"Set plot_spec['group_only_curves']=True to silence, or bin_continuous=True to bin the variable."
                            )
                        grouping_col = None
            
            # Create final grouping column"""
    if old not in s:
        raise SystemExit('old block not found')
    s = s.replace(old, new, 1)
    nb['cells'][i]['source'] = [ln if ln.endswith('\n') else ln + '\n' for ln in s.splitlines(True)]
    path.write_text(json.dumps(nb, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding='utf-8')
    print('patched cell', i)
    break

python3 << 'PY'
import json
from pathlib import Path
path = Path('/Users/charleslevine/Library/CloudStorage/Dropbox/1-Documents/00- Dissertation/0-Next_Chapter/Code_and_Data/New SQL and PY Code/Cursor Workspace PDE/520_pipeline_cox_working.ipynb')
nb = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))
for i, c in enumerate(nb['cells']):
    if c['cell_type'] != 'code':
        continue
    s = ''.join(c.get('source', []))
    if 'group_only_curves' not in s or 'def prepare_plot_data' not in s:
        continue
    old = """            # Stratify CIF/KM curves by group_by only: keep `variable` for filters/OER logic but omit
            # variable bins from plot_group and legend (e.g. div_name curves without "Bin 1 | ...").
            if plot_spec.get('group_only_curves') and group_col is not None:
                grouping_col = None
            
            # Create final grouping column"""
    new = """            # plot_group must not be group_by | <each distinct float> when variable is continuous and
            # bin_continuous=False (that creates hundreds of spurious curves). Omit variable from the
            # composite when group_only_curves is True, or auto when numeric nunique is large.
            if group_col_for_plot is not None and grouping_col is not None:
                gn = grouping_col
                if gn in working_df.columns and pd.api.types.is_numeric_dtype(working_df[gn]) and not plot_spec.get('bin_continuous', False):
                    nu = int(working_df[gn].nunique())
                    if plot_spec.get('group_only_curves') or nu > 20:
                        if nu > 20 and not plot_spec.get('group_only_curves'):
                            print_v(
                                f"  • Auto: omit `{gn}` from plot_group (nunique={nu}) with bin_continuous=False and "
                                f"group_by={group_col_for_plot!r} — one curve per group. "
                                f"Set plot_spec['group_only_curves']=True to silence, or bin_continuous=True to bin the variable."
                            )
                        grouping_col = None
            
            # Create final grouping column"""
    if old not in s:
        raise SystemExit('old block not found')
    s = s.replace(old, new, 1)
    nb['cells'][i]['source'] = [ln if ln.endswith('\n') else ln + '\n' for ln in s.splitlines(True)]
    path.write_text(json.dumps(nb, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding='utf-8')
    print('patched cell', i)
    break

_**Agent (default)**_

Here’s what was going wrong and what we changed.

### Cause
With **`bin_continuous: False`** and both **`variable`** (e.g. `z_tb_ratio_fwd_snr`) and **`group_by`** (`div_name`), the code set `grouping_col` to the **variable column name**. Building `plot_group` then did:

`div_name.astype(str) + " | " + working_df[z_tb_ratio_fwd_snr].astype(str)`

So the second part was **each officer’s actual z value**, not a bin label. Every distinct **(division, float)** became its own group → on the order of **hundreds of groups** even though there are only **~15 divisions**.

So this wasn’t “15 divs × weird bins”; it was **15 divs × many distinct z values**.

### Why `group_only_curves` might not have saved you
That flag **does** clear `grouping_col` so `plot_group` is only `div_name` — **if** it’s on the plot spec that actually runs (`pipeline_config_div_name.py` + `pip_config_file`, re-run Cell 0, then Cell 11). If Cell 0 still pointed at another config, or the notebook hadn’t been re-run after the edit, you’d still get the `div | float` behavior.

### Fix in the notebook
In **`prepare_plot_data`** (Cell 11) we now **drop the variable from the composite `plot_group`** when:

- `group_by` is in use (`group_col_for_plot` is set),
- `grouping_col` is **numeric**,
- **`bin_continuous` is False**, and  
- either **`group_only_curves` is True**, or **`nunique(variable) > 20`**.

So even without the flag, the **div-by-z continuous** case no longer explodes. If the auto path fires, you’ll see a short **`print_v`** line explaining it.

Re-run **Cell 0** (reload config) and **Cell 11**; you should see **one curve per division** (plus whatever passes `min_group_size`), and diagnostics like **`Group 'Unknown': Promotion CIF...`** without a **`|` + float** suffix.

### When you *do* want `variable | group`
Use **`bin_continuous: True`** (with a sensible **`n_bins`**) so the second part of the label is a **bin**, not a raw float.

---

That tracks. A lot of the “mystery” behavior comes from **which file Cell 0 loads** (`pip_config_file`): anything we added to **`pipeline_config.py`** applies **every** time you use the base config, not only the div-name profile.

**Safer pattern:** keep shared defaults minimal in **`pipeline_config.py`**, and put run-specific stuff (div plots, extra `PLOT_CONFIG['plots']`, etc.) only in **`pipeline_config_div_name.py`** (or another overlay) so the base file stays stable for other runs.

import json
from pathlib import Path
path = Path('/Users/charleslevine/Library/CloudStorage/Dropbox/1-Documents/00- Dissertation/0-Next_Chapter/Code_and_Data/New SQL and PY Code/Cursor Workspace PDE/520_pipeline_cox_working.ipynb')
nb = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))
old = """            # Drop missing or literal 'Unknown' on group_by (e.g. div_name); plot_spec overrides PLOT_CONFIG
            _excl_unk = plot_spec.get('exclude_unknown_div_name')
            if _excl_unk is None:
                _excl_unk = PLOT_CONFIG.get('exclude_unknown_div_name', False)
            _gb = plot_spec.get('group_by')
            if _excl_unk and _gb is not None and _gb in working_df.columns:
                col = working_df[_gb]
                is_unk = col.isna() | col.astype(str).str.strip().str.lower().eq('unknown')
                before_count = len(working_df)
                working_df = working_df[~is_unk].copy()
                removed = before_count - len(working_df)
                if removed > 0:
                    print_v(
                        f"  • Excluded {removed:,} officers with missing or 'Unknown' {_gb!r} "
                        f"(exclude_unknown_div_name=True)"
                    )
                    print_v(f"  • Remaining officers: {len(working_df):,}")
"""
new = """            # Drop missing / placeholder labels on group_by when exclude_unknown_div_name is True
            # (plot_spec key overrides PLOT_CONFIG; use None in plot_spec to inherit PLOT_CONFIG only)
            _excl_unk = plot_spec.get('exclude_unknown_div_name')
            if _excl_unk is None:
                _excl_unk = PLOT_CONFIG.get('exclude_unknown_div_name', False)
            _gb = plot_spec.get('group_by')
            if _excl_unk and _gb is not None and _gb in working_df.columns:
                col = working_df[_gb]
                strv = col.astype('string')
                sl = strv.str.strip().str.lower()
                is_unk = strv.isna() | sl.eq('unknown') | sl.eq('nan') | sl.eq('none') | (sl == '')
                before_count = len(working_df)
                working_df = working_df[~is_unk].copy()
                removed = before_count - len(working_df)
                if removed > 0:
                    print_v(
                        f"  • Excluded {removed:,} officers with missing/Unknown/placeholder {_gb!r} "
                        f"(exclude_unknown_div_name=True)"
                    )
                    print_v(f"  • Remaining officers: {len(working_df):,}")
"""
for i, c in enumerate(nb['cells']):
    if c['cell_type'] != 'code':
        continue
    s = ''.join(c.get('source', []))
    if old not in s:
        continue
    s = s.replace(old, new, 1)
    nb['cells'][i]['source'] = [ln if ln.endswith('\n') else ln + '\n' for ln in s.splitlines(True)]
    path.write_text(json.dumps(nb, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding='utf-8')
    print('patched cell', i)
    break
else:
    print('OLD BLOCK NOT FOUND')

"""
new = """            # Drop missing / placeholder labels on group_by when exclude_unknown_div_name is True
            # (plot_spec key overrides PLOT_CONFIG; use None in plot_spec to inherit PLOT_CONFIG only)
            _excl_unk = plot_spec.get('exclude_unknown_div_name')
            if _excl_unk is None:

                    )

"""
for i, c in enumerate(nb['cells']):

    s = s.replace(old, new, 1)

_**Agent (default)**_

Here’s what was already there and what we tightened up.

### Boolean (already present)
- **`exclude_unknown_div_name`** in **`PLOT_CONFIG`** (`pipeline_config.py`, default **`False`**) — global default for Cell 11.
- **`prepare_plot_data`** in **`520_pipeline_cox_working.ipynb` (Cell 11)** — if this is **`True`**, it drops rows where **`group_by`** is missing or looks like an unknown/placeholder label.

Per-plot you can set **`'exclude_unknown_div_name': False`** on a spec (it overrides the default for that plot only).

### What was going wrong
If **`RUN_CR_PLOT_DEFAULTS`** did **not** include **`exclude_unknown_div_name: True`**, only the one plot dict that set it would exclude Unknown; anything built only from defaults could still keep **Unknown**.

### What we changed just now
1. **`pipeline_config_div_name.py`** — set **`'exclude_unknown_div_name': True`** inside **`RUN_CR_PLOT_DEFAULTS`**, so **every** CR plot in that profile excludes unknown/missing **`group_by`** whenever **`group_by`** is set (plots with **`group_by: None`** are unchanged). Removed the duplicate key from the TB-by-div entry and left a short comment there.
2. **`520_pipeline_cox_working.ipynb` (Cell 11)** — filter now treats as excluded: **NaN**, **`unknown`**, **`nan`**, **`none`** (case-insensitive), and **blank** strings after strip.

### Transcription checklist for your other machine
- Cell 11: the **`exclude_unknown_div_name`** block in **`prepare_plot_data`** (updated logic above).
- **`pipeline_config.py`**: **`PLOT_CONFIG['exclude_unknown_div_name']`** (optional global).
- **`pipeline_config_div_name.py`**: **`exclude_unknown_div_name: True`** in **`RUN_CR_PLOT_DEFAULTS`** (or set **`True`** on each plot that uses **`div_name`**).

To **show Unknown again** for a run: set **`RUN_CR_PLOT_DEFAULTS['exclude_unknown_div_name'] = False`**, or **`False`** on a single plot spec. Reload config (**Cell 0**) and rerun **Cell 11**.

```python
            # ===== exclude_unknown_div_name (search this) — PLOT_CONFIG or per-plot spec =====
            # Drop missing / placeholder labels on group_by when exclude_unknown_div_name is True
            # (plot_spec key overrides PLOT_CONFIG; use None in plot_spec to inherit PLOT_CONFIG only)
            _excl_unk = plot_spec.get('exclude_unknown_div_name')
            if _excl_unk is None:
                _excl_unk = PLOT_CONFIG.get('exclude_unknown_div_name', False)
            _gb = plot_spec.get('group_by')
            if _excl_unk and _gb is not None and _gb in working_df.columns:
                col = working_df[_gb]
                strv = col.astype('string')
                sl = strv.str.strip().str.lower()
                is_unk = strv.isna() | sl.eq('unknown') | sl.eq('nan') | sl.eq('none') | (sl == '')
                before_count = len(working_df)
                working_df = working_df[~is_unk].copy()
                removed = before_count - len(working_df)
                if removed > 0:
                    print_v(
                        f"  • Excluded {removed:,} officers with missing/Unknown/placeholder {_gb!r} "
                        f"(exclude_unknown_div_name=True)"
                    )
                    print_v(f"  • Remaining officers: {len(working_df):,}")
```

Add after seaborn block:

```python
from typing import Any, Callable, Optional


def apply_unknown_group_by_filter(
    working_df: pd.DataFrame,
    plot_spec: dict,
    plot_config: dict,
    log: Optional[Callable[[str], None]] = None,
) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """
    When ``exclude_unknown_div_name`` is True in ``plot_spec`` or ``plot_config``
    (plot_spec wins when not None), drop officer rows where ``group_by`` is NaN
    or a placeholder label: 'unknown', 'nan', 'none', or blank (case-insensitive).
    """
    log = log or (lambda _m: None)
    excl = plot_spec.get("exclude_unknown_div_name")
    if excl is None:
        excl = plot_config.get("exclude_unknown_div_name", False)
    gb = plot_spec.get("group_by")
    if not excl or gb is None or gb not in working_df.columns:
        return working_df
    col = working_df[gb]
    strv = col.astype("string")
    sl = strv.str.strip().str.lower()
    is_bad = strv.isna() | sl.eq("unknown") | sl.eq("nan") | sl.eq("none") | (sl == "")
    before = len(working_df)
    out = working_df[~is_bad].copy()
    removed = before - len(out)
    if removed > 0:
        log(
            f"  • Dropped {removed:,} officers with missing/Unknown/placeholder {gb!r} "
            f"(exclude_unknown_div_name=True)"
        )
        log(f"  • Remaining officers: {len(out):,}")
    return out
```

I'll use simpler typing without importing Callable if we use Any - actually use Callable from typing.

                working_df = working_df[~is_unk].copy()
                removed = before_count - len(working_df)
                if removed > 0:
                    print_v(
                        f"  • Excluded {removed:,} officers with missing/Unknown/placeholder {_gb!r} "
                        f"(exclude_unknown_div_name=True)"
                    )
                    print_v(f"  • Remaining officers: {len(working_df):,}")
            
            """
        if old2 in s:
            s = s.replace(old2, new_block, 1)
        else:
            raise SystemExit('exclude block not found')
    else:
        s = s.replace(old_block, new_block, 1)

    nb['cells'][i]['source'] = [ln if ln.endswith('\n') else ln + '\n' for ln in s.splitlines(True)]
    path.write_text(json.dumps(nb, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding='utf-8')
    print('updated cell', i)
    break
PY
```

```
updated cell 51

```
</details>
</tool-use>

_**Agent (default)**_

## Why the file looked “obsolete”

Common pattern:

1. **Something else wrote the file on disk** (another Cursor window, a merge, Dropbox syncing an older/newer version, or Jupyter saving).
2. **Your editor tab kept the in-memory copy** until you reloaded, so search and line numbers didn’t match disk.

**Practical habits:** after big edits from another app, use **Revert / reload from disk** on that tab; if you use Jupyter and Cursor on the same notebook, avoid having both unsaved; watch for **Dropbox conflict copies** if paths diverge.

---

## Where the “Unknown” fix actually lives (one flow)

Think of it as **one implementation**, **one call site**, **two config layers**.

| Layer | File | Role |
|--------|------|------|
| **Logic** | `cox_plot_helpers.py` | `apply_unknown_group_by_filter(...)` reads `exclude_unknown_div_name` from **`plot_spec` first**, then **`plot_config`** (your merged `PLOT_CONFIG`), and drops bad `group_by` rows when enabled. |

```27:66:cox_plot_helpers.py
def apply_unknown_group_by_filter(
    working_df: pd.DataFrame,
    plot_spec: dict,
    plot_config: dict,
    log: Optional[Callable[[str], None]] = None,
) -> pd.DataFrame:
    ...
    excl = plot_spec.get("exclude_unknown_div_name")
    if excl is None:
        excl = plot_config.get("exclude_unknown_div_name", False)
    ...
```

| Layer | File | Role |
|--------|------|------|
| **Default for all plots** | `pipeline_config.py` → `PLOT_CONFIG` | Global default **`exclude_unknown_div_name`: `False`** so you don’t silently drop rows unless you opt in. |

```839:842:pipeline_config.py
    'cif_bar_show_xlabels': False,   # CIF bar-panel x-axis label ("Bin")
    # If True, KM/CR drops rows where group_by is NaN or placeholder Unknown (title case from merge; any case matched)
    'exclude_unknown_div_name': False,
```

| Layer | File | Role |
|--------|------|------|
| **Per-plot override** | `pipeline_config_div_name.py` | Only the specs that need it set **`'exclude_unknown_div_name': True`** (e.g. the div-name CR plot). |

```108:112:pipeline_config_div_name.py
            'bin_continuous': False,  # skip useless binning; see group_only_curves
            'group_by': 'div_name',
            'group_only_curves': True,
            'exclude_unknown_div_name': True,  # omit Unknown/missing div for this plot only; base PLOT_CONFIG default is False
            'cr_color_mode': 'set1',  # discrete colors like legacy KM; default gradient = blue/orange ramps
```

| Layer | File | Role |
|--------|------|------|
| **Wiring** | `520` CELL 11 → `prepare_plot_data` | Imports `apply_unknown_group_by_filter` and calls it with **`(working_df, plot_spec, PLOT_CONFIG, print_v)`** after the `filter_nan_group_by` block. You **don’t** reimplement the rule in 520; you only ensure this call stays there. |

---

## Mental model

- **Change behavior (what counts as Unknown, how we filter):** edit **`cox_plot_helpers.py`** only.  
- **Change default for every plot:** edit **`pipeline_config.py`** `PLOT_CONFIG`.  
- **Turn it on for one run/spec:** edit that entry in **`pipeline_config_div_name.py`** (or whatever module supplies `plot_spec`).  
- **Ensure the pipeline runs it:** **`520`** CELL 11 must keep the **import + one line** in `prepare_plot_data`.

You do **not** need the string `exclude_unknown_div_name` duplicated all over 520; the comment next to the call is enough for search, as long as the configs set the flag where you want it.

If you want, we can next add a **one-line markdown note** under the CELL 11 heading in 520 that points to these four places so future-you finds it without grep gymnastics.

---

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 474,
            "text": "        "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 475,
            "text": "        # Simple bar chart for now (can be enhanced with time-dependent analysis)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 476,
            "text": "        ax1.bar([f'Group {group}'], [promotion_rate], color=colors[i], alpha=0.7)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 477,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 478,
            "text": "    ax1.set_title(f'Promotion Rates by Group {title_suffix}')"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 479,
            "text": "    ax1.set_ylabel('Promotion Rate')"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 480,
            "text": "    ax1.set_ylim(0, 1)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 481,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 482,
            "text": "    # Plot 2: Event Distribution"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 483,
            "text": "    event_counts = officer_data.groupby([group_col, 'event']).size().unstack(fill_value=0)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 484,
            "text": "    event_counts.plot(kind='bar', ax=ax2, color=['red', 'green'])"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 485,
            "text": "    ax2.set_title(f'Event Distribution {title_suffix}')"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 486,
            "text": "    ax2.set_ylabel('Number of Officers')"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 487,
            "text": "    ax2.legend(['Not Promoted', 'Promoted'])"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 488,
            "text": "    ax2.tick_params(axis='x', rotation=45)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 489,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 490,
            "text": "    # Plot 3: Time to Event Distribution"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 491,
            "text": "    for i, group in enumerate(groups):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 492,
            "text": "        group_data = officer_data[officer_data[group_col] == group]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 493,
            "text": "        if len(group_data) \u003e 0:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 494,
            "text": "            ax3.hist(group_data['survival_days'], alpha=0.6, label=f'Group {group}', "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 495,
            "text": "                    color=colors[i], bins=20)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 496,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 497,
            "text": "    ax3.set_title(f'Time to Event Distribution {title_suffix}')"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 531
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 14,
            "line": 443
          }

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 874,
            "text": "def plot_competing_risks_cif_bars("
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 875,
            "text": "    plot_df,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 876,
            "text": "    plot_spec,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 877,
            "text": "    metadata,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 878,
            "text": "    output_dir=None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 879,
            "text": "    filename_base=None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 880,
            "text": "    dpi=400,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 881,
            "text": "    figsize=(10, 9),"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 882,
            "text": "    tick_sz=None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 883,
            "text": "    legend_outside=True,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 884,
            "text": "):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 966
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 967
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 968,
            "text": "    def _build_hex_gradient(hex_colors, n_colors):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 969,
            "text": "        if not hex_colors:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 970,
            "text": "            return None"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 971,
            "text": "        if len(hex_colors) == n_colors:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 972,
            "text": "            return hex_colors"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 973,
            "text": "        if len(hex_colors) == 1:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 974,
            "text": "            return [hex_colors[0]] * n_colors"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 975,
            "text": "        # Interpolate between first and last colors"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 976,
            "text": "        start = _hex_to_rgb(hex_colors[0])"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 977,
            "text": "        end = _hex_to_rgb(hex_colors[-1])"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 978,
            "text": "        gradient = []"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 979,
            "text": "        for i in range(n_colors):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 980,
            "text": "            t = i / max(n_colors - 1, 1)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 981,
            "text": "            rgb = ("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 982,
            "text": "                start[0] + (end[0] - start[0]) * t,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 983,
            "text": "                start[1] + (end[1] - start[1]) * t,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 984,
            "text": "                start[2] + (end[2] - start[2]) * t,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 985,
            "text": "            )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 986,
            "text": "            gradient.append(_rgb_to_hex(rgb))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 987,
            "text": "        return gradient"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 988
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 989,
            "text": "    promo_palette = plot_spec.get(\"cif_bar_palette_promo\") or plot_spec.get(\"cif_bar_palette\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 990,
            "text": "    attr_palette = plot_spec.get(\"cif_bar_palette_attr\") or plot_spec.get(\"cif_bar_palette\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 991
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 992,
            "text": "    promo_palette = _build_hex_gradient(promo_palette or [\"#d7e6f5\", \"#1f5aa6\"], len(q_labels))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 993,
            "text": "    attr_palette = _build_hex_gradient(attr_palette or [\"#fde2cf\", \"#c0581a\"], len(q_labels))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 994
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 995,
            "text": "    # Build color maps aligned to ordered groups so bar colors match CR curves"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 996,
            "text": "    ordered_groups = [gs[\"group\"] for gs in group_stats]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 997,
            "text": "    promo_color_map = {g: promo_palette[i] for i, g in enumerate(ordered_groups)}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 998,
            "text": "    attr_color_map = {g: attr_palette[i] for i, g in enumerate(ordered_groups)}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 999,
            "text": "    promo_colors = [promo_color_map.get(gs[\"group\"], \"#4c4c4c\") for gs in group_stats]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1000,
            "text": "    attr_colors = [attr_color_map.get(gs[\"group\"], \"#4c4c4c\") for gs in group_stats]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1001,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1002,
            "text": "    promo_df = pd.DataFrame({\"bin\": q_labels, \"cif\": promo_vals})"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1003,
            "text": "    attr_df = pd.DataFrame({\"bin\": q_labels, \"cif\": attr_vals})"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1004,
            "text": "    promo_df[\"bin\"] = pd.Categorical(promo_df[\"bin\"], categories=q_labels, ordered=True)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1005,
            "text": "    attr_df[\"bin\"] = pd.Categorical(attr_df[\"bin\"], categories=q_labels, ordered=True)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1006
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1007,
            "text": "    if _SEABORN_AVAILABLE:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1008,
            "text": "        sns.barplot"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 1116
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 873
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./cox_plot_helpers.py",
        "signatures": {

                "column": 5,

              "startPosition": {
                "column": 1,

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 91,
            "text": "USE_OVERRIDE_CR_PLOTS = True"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 92
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 93,
            "text": "if USE_OVERRIDE_CR_PLOTS:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 94,
            "text": "    # Variable names in data: z_ when RUN_SCALE='z', else raw"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 95,
            "text": "    # Individual CR plots (edit per-plot here; common settings come from RUN_CR_PLOT_DEFAULTS)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 96,
            "text": "    _cr_var_tb = Z_TB_RATIO_SNR_COL if RUN_SCALE == 'z' else TB_RATIO_SNR_COL"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 97,
            "text": "    _cr_var_pool = ("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 98,
            "text": "        f'{Z_PREFIX}{POOL_MINUS_MEAN_SNR_COL}' if RUN_SCALE == 'z' else POOL_MINUS_MEAN_SNR_COL"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 99,
            "text": "    )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 100,
            "text": "    PLOT_CONFIG['plots'] = ["
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 101,
            "text": "        {**RUN_CR_PLOT_DEFAULTS, 'variable': _cr_var_tb, 'name': 'run1_cr_' + _cr_var_tb},"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 102,
            "text": "        {**RUN_CR_PLOT_DEFAULTS, 'variable': _cr_var_pool, 'name': 'run1_cr_' + _cr_var_pool},"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 103,
            "text": "        {**RUN_CR_PLOT_DEFAULTS, 'variable': 'star_pool_interaction', 'name': 'run1_cr_star_pool_interaction'},"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 104,
            "text": "        # TB ratio (z or raw) for filter_nan_variable / OER filters; curves = one per div_name (no variable bin)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 105,
            "text": "        {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 106,
            "text": "            **RUN_CR_PLOT_DEFAULTS,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 107,
            "text": "            'variable': _cr_var_tb,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 108,
            "text": "            'bin_continuous': False,  # skip useless binning; see group_only_curves"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 109,
            "text": "            'group_by': 'div_name',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 110,
            "text": "            'group_only_curves': True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 111,
            "text": "            'exclude_unknown_div_name': True,  # omit Unknown/missing div for this plot only; base PLOT_CONFIG default is False"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 112,
            "text": "            'cr_color_mode': 'set1',  # discrete colors like legacy KM; default gradient = blue/orange ramps"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 113,
            "text": "            'name': 'run1_cr_tb_by_div',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 114,
            "text": "        },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 115,
            "text": "    ]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 116
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 117,
            "text": "# Filtering parameters for RUN 1"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 118,
            "text": "CA = ['IN', 'AR', 'EN', 'AV', 'FA', 'SF', 'AD']  # 7 branches"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 119,
            "text": "# CA = ['IN', 'AR', 'EN', 'AV', 'FA', 'AD']  # 6 branches (omit SF)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 120,
            "text": "CS = ['CM', 'CA', 'MI', 'MP', 'SC']"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 121,
            "text": "CSS = ['AG', 'FI', 'OD', 'QM', 'TC', 'LG']"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 122
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 123,
            "text": "filtering_params['filter_dates'] = {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 124,
            "text": "    'enabled': False,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 125,
            "text": "    'start_date': '2000-01-01',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 126,
            "text": "    'end_date': '2022-06-30',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 127,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 128,
            "text": "filtering_params['filter_job_codes'] = {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 129,
            "text": "    'enabled': True,"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 20,
            "line": 128
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 117,
            "line": 89
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./pipeline_config_div_name.py",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3288590908050537
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 971,
            "text": "    'plots': ["
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 972,
            "text": "        # Add plot specifications here"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 973,
            "text": "        # Each plot is a dictionary with required keys:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 974,
            "text": "        #   - 'name': str (unique identifier, used internally and in metadata)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 975,
            "text": "        #              NOTE: Actual filename is auto-generated with enhanced description"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 976,
            "text": "        #   - 'variable': str or None (column name to analyze, must be in COLUMN_CONFIG, or None for grouping only)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 977,
            "text": "        #   - 'group_by': str or None (column name to group by, creates separate curves, or None)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 978,
            "text": "        #   - 'plot_type': str ('kaplan_meier' or 'competing_risks')"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 979,
            "text": "        #   - 'bin_continuous': bool (True for continuous variables, False for categorical)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 980,
            "text": "        #   - 'n_bins': int (number of bins, ignored if bin_continuous=False)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 981,
            "text": "        #   - 'bin_method': str ('quantile' or 'equal_width', ignored if bin_continuous=False)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 982,
            "text": "        #   - 'min_group_size': int (minimum officers per group)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 983,
            "text": "        #   - 'use_time_varying': bool (True for time-varying vars, False for static, default True)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 984,
            "text": "        #   - 'filter_zero_oer': bool (True to exclude officers with no OER data, only for OER variables)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 985,
            "text": "        #   - 'filter_nan_variable': bool (True to drop rows where variable is NaN)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 986,
            "text": "        #   - 'filter_nan_group_by': bool (True to drop rows where group_by is NaN)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 987,
            "text": "        #   - 'strict_equal_n': bool (force equal-N bins with rank-based qcut)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 988,
            "text": "        #   - 'x_max_days': int or None (cap x-axis at N days)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 989,
            "text": "        #   - 'bin_group_by': bool (True to bin group_by separately)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 990,
            "text": "        #   - 'group_bins': int (number of bins for group_by, defaults to n_bins)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 991,
            "text": "        #   - 'group_bin_method': str ('quantile' or 'equal_width' for group_by)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 992,
            "text": "        #"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 993,
            "text": "        # OUTPUT FILES:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 994,
            "text": "        #   - Plot: {plot_type}_{variable}_{group_by}_{bins}_{method}_{filter}_{dates}.png"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 995,
            "text": "        #   - Metadata: {plot_type}_{variable}_{group_by}_{bins}_{method}_{filter}_{dates}_metadata.json"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 996,
            "text": "        ########################################################################################################################################"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 997,
            "text": "        {    # 1: TB ratio (mode-specific, raw)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 998,
            "text": "            'variable': TB_RATIO_SNR_COL,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 999,
            "text": "            'name': 'cr_' + TB_RATIO_SNR_COL,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1000,
            "text": "            'group_by': None,"
          },
          {

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 3639,
            "text": "    \"# Customize promotion plot\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3640,
            "text": "    \"ax1.set_title('Cumulative Incidence of Promotion\\\\nby Year Group', fontsize=title_sz, fontweight='bold')\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3641,
            "text": "    \"ax1.set_xlabel('Time (days)', fontsize=axis_sz)\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3642,
            "text": "    \"ax1.set_ylabel('Promotion Probability', fontsize=axis_sz)\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3643,
            "text": "    \"ax1.grid(True, alpha=0.3)\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3644,
            "text": "    \"ax1.legend(title='Year Group', loc='best', fontsize=lgnd_sz)\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3645,
            "text": "    \"ax1.set_ylim(0, 1)\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3646,
            "text": "    \"ax1.tick_params(axis='both', which='major', labelsize=tick_sz)\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3647,
            "text": "    \"insert_pz_line(ax1, pz_ref_line, 0, fnt_sz, ref_ln_label_sz, c1, c2)\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3648,
            "text": "    \"insert_az_line(ax1, pz_ref_line, 0, fnt_sz, ref_ln_label_sz, c3, c4)\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3649,
            "text": "    \"\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3650,
            "text": "    \"# Customize attrition plot\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3651,
            "text": "    \"ax2.set_title('Cumulative Incidence of Attrition\\\\nby Year Group', fontsize=title_sz, fontweight='bold')\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3652,
            "text": "    \"ax2.set_xlabel('Time (days)', fontsize=axis_sz)\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3653,
            "text": "    \"ax2.set_ylabel('Attrition Probability', fontsize=axis_sz)\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3654,
            "text": "    \"ax2.grid(True, alpha=0.3)\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3655,
            "text": "    \"ax2.legend(title='Year Group', loc='best', fontsize=lgnd_sz)\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3656,
            "text": "    \"ax2.set_ylim(0, 1)\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3657,
            "text": "    \"ax2.tick_params(axis='both', which='major', labelsize=tick_sz)\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3658,
            "text": "    \"insert_pz_line(ax2, pz_ref_line, 0, fnt_sz, ref_ln_label_sz, c1, c2)\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3659,
            "text": "    \"insert_az_line(ax2, pz_ref_line, 0, fnt_sz, ref_ln_label_sz, c3, c4)\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3660,
            "text": "    \"    \\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3661,
            "text": "    \"plt.tight_layout()\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3662,
            "text": "    \"plt.savefig(plot_dir + '/competing_risks_yg.png', dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3663,
            "text": "    \"print(\\\"✅ Competing risks plot saved: plots/competing_risks_yg.png\\\")\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3664,
            "text": "    \"plt.show()\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3665,
            "text": "    \"\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3666,
            "text": "    \"# === 10.5. COMPETING RISKS ANALYSIS BY FINAL JOB CODE ===\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3667,
            "text": "    \"print(\\\"\\\\n🔍 Competing Risks Analysis by Final Job Code:\\\")\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3668,
            "text": "    \"\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3669,
            "text": "    \"# Create figure for Final Job Code analysis\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3670,
            "text": "    \"fig, (ax1, ax2) = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=fig_sz)\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3671,
            "text": "    \"\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3672,
            "text": "    \"# Get unique Final Job Codes\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3673,
            "text": "    \"final_job_codes = officer_data['final_job_code'].unique()\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3674,
            "text": "    \"\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3675,
            "text": "    \"# Choose Final Job Codes\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3676,
            "text": "    \"final_job_codes = ['IN','AR','FA','SC','AV','AD']\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3677,
            "text": "    \"print(f\\\"📊 Final Job Codes chosen: {final_job_codes}\\\")\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3678,
            "text": "    \"\\n\","
          }
        ],

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 3752,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3753
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3754,
            "text": "**Cursor**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3755
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3756,
            "text": "Two separate issues here — I addressed the **color mismatch** and I’ll give you a concrete diagnostic path for the **“narrow distribution”** concern."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3757
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3758,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3759
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3760,
            "text": "## ✅ 1) CR curve colors now match bar colors"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3761,
            "text": "I updated &#96;plot_competing_risks_cif_bars()&#96; so the bar colors are **mapped directly to the same ordered groups** used for the CR curves (by mean ascending). This makes Q1–Qn bars match the corresponding CR bin colors."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3762
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3763,
            "text": "Updated file:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3764,
            "text": "- &#96;cox_plot_helpers.py&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3765
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3766,
            "text": "What changed:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3767,
            "text": "- Build &#96;ordered_groups&#96; from the same sorted bin order"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3768,
            "text": "- Build &#96;promo_color_map&#96; / &#96;attr_color_map&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3769,
            "text": "- Use those mapped colors in the bar plots"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3770
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3771,
            "text": "So now: **CR lines ↔ bar colors should match**."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3772
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3773,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3774
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3775,
            "text": "## 2) Why your CR curves look “too narrow”"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3776,
            "text": "The plots showing very tight spreads are almost always caused by one of these:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3777
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3778,
            "text": "### A) You’re still plotting old values"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3779,
            "text": "Even after code changes, **Cell 11 can still be using cached data**.  "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3780,
            "text": "If the ratio was previously 0–100 and now 0–1, you must re‑run from **Cell 4 → 11**."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3781
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3782,
            "text": "### B) Binning method changed"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3783,
            "text": "Your screenshots show **equal width bins** in one case and **quantile bins** in the other.  "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3784,
            "text": "Equal width on a skewed 0–1 distribution can collapse most officers into the same region, which makes curves look “compressed.”"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3785
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3786,
            "text": "### C) The filter set changed"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3787,
            "text": "Your N dropped from ~18,999 to ~16,343 between runs, which means **filtering changed** (job codes, YGs, etc.).  "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3788,
            "text": "That alone can materially change the curves."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3789
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3790,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3791
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3792,
            "text": "## What I recommend you do next (quick checks)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3793
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3794,
            "text": "### 1) Verify the variable scale **before plotting**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3795,
            "text": "In Cell 11, add a quick check:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3796,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;python"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3797,
            "text": "print(df_plot['tb_ratio_bwd_snr'].describe())"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3798,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3799,
            "text": "If max \u003e 1.0, you’re still using old cached values."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3800
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3801,
            "text": "### 2) Confirm binning method for this plot"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3802,
            "text": "Check the &#96;PLOT_CONFIG&#96; entry for that plot and ensure:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3803,
            "text": "- &#96;bin_method: 'quantile'&#96; if you want evenly sized groups"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3804
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3805,
            "text": "### 3) Re‑run from Cell 4 once"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3806,
            "text": "That guarantees the new 0–1 ratios are carried all the way through."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3807
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3808,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3809
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3810,
            "text": "If you want, I can also add a **one‑line “scale check”** print for any tb_ratio variable in Cell 11 so the plot warns you if values look like 0–100."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3811
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3812,
            "text": "Thanks — the &#96;describe()&#96; confirms the **ratio is now 0–1**. ✅"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 3813
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 3750
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./20260309_cursor_work_review.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3082214593887329
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "from pipeline_config import *"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "# RUN 3: Individual TB ratio vs pool rank (percentile), interactive surface."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "# Set RUN_SCALE to 'raw' or 'z'; Run 3 uses z by default for apples-to-apples."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "RUN_SCALE = 'z'"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "# One-swipe defaults for all CR plots (used by automated plots and by individual plots when USE_OVERRIDE_CR_PLOTS is True)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "RUN_CR_PLOT_DEFAULTS = {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "    'group_by': None,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "    'plot_type': 'competing_risks',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "    'bin_continuous': True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "    'n_bins': 8,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "    'bin_method': 'quantile',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "    'min_group_size': 3,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "    'use_time_varying': True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "    'filter_zero_oer': True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "    'filter_nan_variable': True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "    'filter_nan_group_by': True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "    'strict_equal_n': False,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "    'x_max_days': 4500,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "    'bin_group_by': True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "    'group_bins': 3,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "    'group_bin_method': 'equal_width',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "# Quadratics: set in one place here via quadratic_bases. Order of operations (in Cell 10.5): raw → square → then z-score the square if RUN_SCALE is 'z'."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "RUN_PROFILE = {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "    'tv_vars': [TB_RATIO_SNR_COL, POOL_TB_RATIO_RANK_PCT_SNR_COL],"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "    'quadratic_bases': [POOL_TB_RATIO_RANK_PCT_SNR_COL],"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "    'interaction_name': 'standing_pool_interaction',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "    'plot_prefix': 'run3',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "    'var_x_label': 'TB ratio (z)',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "    'var_y_label': 'Pool TB ratio rank pct (z)',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "    'cr_plot_defaults': RUN_CR_PLOT_DEFAULTS,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "globals().update(expand_run_profile(RUN_SCALE, RUN_PROFILE))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "# Use explicit CR plots below only when True; when False (default), automated plots from expand_run_profile() are used"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "USE_OVERRIDE_CR_PLOTS = False"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "# USE_OVERRIDE_CR_PLOTS = True   # example: set True to use the explicit individual CR plots below (edit per-plot as needed)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "if USE_OVERRIDE_CR_PLOTS:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "    # Individual CR plots (z-scale; edit per-plot here if needed). Example: {**RUN_CR_PLOT_DEFAULTS, 'n_bins': 5, 'variable': Z_TB_RATIO_SNR_COL, 'name': 'run3_cr_' + Z_TB_RATIO_SNR_COL}"
          }
        ],

            "line": 43
          },

            "lineNumber": 4489,

          },
          {

          },
