    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 546,
            "text": "- Builds &#96;df_analysis&#96; from basic_demographic_cols, static_cols, time_varying_cols, and **model_time_varying_cols** (so interaction/quadratic columns from the run config are available for CR plots)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 547
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 548,
            "text": "**Cell 12: Cox regression**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 549,
            "text": "- 12.1 prepares data and validates structure."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 550,
            "text": "- 12.2 fits static model."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 551,
            "text": "- 12.3 fits full model (static + time varying)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 552,
            "text": "- 12.4 compares models and signal ratios."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 553,
            "text": "- 12.5 competing risks analysis."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 554,
            "text": "- 12.6 partial effects plots."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 555,
            "text": "- 12.6A combined/diagnostic effect plots."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 556,
            "text": "- 12.7 interaction diagnostics (placeholder structure when applicable)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 557
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 558,
            "text": "## Plotting System (Cell 11)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 559
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 560,
            "text": "### Competing Risks and CIF Bar Plots"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 561,
            "text": "- CIF bar charts are generated by &#96;plot_competing_risks_cif_bars()&#96; in &#96;cox_plot_helpers.py&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 562,
            "text": "- Bar colors can be defined in plot spec using hex palettes:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 563,
            "text": "  - &#96;cif_bar_palette&#96; (shared)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 564,
            "text": "  - &#96;cif_bar_palette_promo&#96; / &#96;cif_bar_palette_attr&#96; (separate)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 565,
            "text": "- Line plots use the same palette mapping so colors match between line and bar plots."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 566
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 567,
            "text": "### Plot Flags"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 568,
            "text": "- &#96;plot_cif_bars&#96;: enable CIF bars on a CR plot spec"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 569,
            "text": "- &#96;cif_bar_show_legend&#96;: toggle legend for CIF bars"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 570,
            "text": "- &#96;cif_bar_legend_outside&#96;: legend placement"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 571,
            "text": "- &#96;include_z_cols&#96;: auto include z cols for plotting when enabled"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 572,
            "text": "- &#96;log_x&#96;, &#96;log_y&#96;: optional log scaling in &#96;plot_var_distribution()&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 573
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 574,
            "text": "## Quadratic Terms and CIF Bar Alignment"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 575
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 576,
            "text": "When the CIF bar charts show a U shape or inverted U shape not captured by a linear term, add a **quadratic term** to the Cox model:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 577
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 578,
            "text": "1. Use the **raw** base variable (not z scored) to create the squared term."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 579,
            "text": "2. Standardize the squared term if standardization is enabled."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 580,
            "text": "3. Include both linear and squared terms in the model to allow curvature."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 581
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 582,
            "text": "This is handled in Cell 10.5 via the interaction/standardization pipeline and validated in Cell 12.6A with combined effect plots (linear + quadratic) to compare the model curve against the CIF bar shape. **Cell 12.6A** detects the linear+quadratic pair from &#96;final_model_vars&#96; (or optional &#96;COMBINED_EFFECT_BASE_COL&#96; in config) so it works for any run (raw or z, pool minus mean or pool rank)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 583
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 584,
            "text": "## Performance Characteristics (Typical)"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 585
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 545
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./current_documents/520_PIPELINE_COX_OVERVIEW.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3689596652984619
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 934,
            "text": "def plot_competing_risks_cif_bars("
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 935,
            "text": "    plot_df,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 936,
            "text": "    plot_spec,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 937,
            "text": "    metadata,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 938,
            "text": "    output_dir=None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 939,
            "text": "    filename_base=None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 940,
            "text": "    dpi=400,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 941,
            "text": "    figsize=(10, 9),"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 942,
            "text": "    tick_sz=None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 943,
            "text": "    legend_outside=True,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 944,
            "text": "):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 945,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 946,
            "text": "    Plot bar charts of final CIF by bin for promotion and attrition."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 947
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 948,
            "text": "    Bars are ordered by the mean of the plotted variable (ascending),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 949,
            "text": "    which yields z-score ascending order when plotting z-scored variables."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 950
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 951,
            "text": "    If &#96;&#96;plot_spec['cr_color_mode']&#96;&#96; is one of &#96;&#96;set1&#96;&#96; / &#96;&#96;tab20&#96;&#96; / &#96;&#96;husl&#96;&#96; / &#96;&#96;glasbey&#96;&#96;,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 952,
            "text": "    bar colors match the competing-risks curves (via &#96;&#96;cr_discrete_group_colors&#96;&#96;). Otherwise"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 953,
            "text": "    the blue/orange gradient from &#96;&#96;cif_bar_palette*&#96;&#96; is used."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 954
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 955,
            "text": "    &#96;&#96;plot_spec['cif_bar_xtick_labels']&#96;&#96;: &#96;&#96;'group'&#96;&#96; (division names), &#96;&#96;'quantile'&#96;&#96; (Q1..Qn),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 956,
            "text": "    or &#96;&#96;None&#96;&#96; for auto: group labels when &#96;&#96;group_only_curves&#96;&#96; and &#96;&#96;group_by&#96;&#96; are set."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 957,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 958,
            "text": "    if plot_df is None or plot_df.empty:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 959,
            "text": "        print(\"⚠️ No data for CIF bar plots.\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 960,
            "text": "        return None"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 961
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 962,
            "text": "    var_name = plot_spec.get(\"variable\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 963,
            "text": "    group_col = \"plot_group\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 964,
            "text": "    if group_col not in plot_df.columns:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 965,
            "text": "        print(\"⚠️ plot_group missing; cannot build CIF bars.\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 966,
            "text": "        return None"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 967
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 968,
            "text": "    # Compute per-group stats"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 969,
            "text": "    group_stats = []"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 970,
            "text": "    for group, gdf in plot_df.groupby(group_col):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 971,
            "text": "        # Match competing-risks filtering: require valid stop time"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 1205
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 22,
            "line": 930
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./cox_plot_helpers.py",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.36771079897880554
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 832,
            "text": "def plot_competing_risks_cif_bars("
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 833,
            "text": "    plot_df,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 834,
            "text": "    plot_spec,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 835,
            "text": "    metadata,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 836,
            "text": "    output_dir=None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 837,
            "text": "    filename_base=None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 838,
            "text": "    dpi=300,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 839,
            "text": "    figsize=(10, 9),"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 840,
            "text": "    legend_outside=True,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 841,
            "text": "):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 842,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 843,
            "text": "    Plot bar charts of final CIF by bin for promotion and attrition."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 844
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 845,
            "text": "    Bars are ordered by the mean of the plotted variable (ascending),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 846,
            "text": "    which yields z-score ascending order when plotting z-scored variables."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 847,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 848,
            "text": "    if plot_df is None or plot_df.empty:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 849,
            "text": "        print(\"⚠️ No data for CIF bar plots.\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 850,
            "text": "        return None"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 851
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 852,
            "text": "    var_name = plot_spec.get(\"variable\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 853,
            "text": "    group_col = \"plot_group\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 854,
            "text": "    if group_col not in plot_df.columns:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 855,
            "text": "        print(\"⚠️ plot_group missing; cannot build CIF bars.\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 856,
            "text": "        return None"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 857
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 858,
            "text": "    # Compute per-group stats"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 859,
            "text": "    group_stats = []"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 860,
            "text": "    for group, gdf in plot_df.groupby(group_col):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 861,
            "text": "        # Match competing-risks filtering: require valid stop time"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 862,
            "text": "        if \"stop_time\" in gdf.columns:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 863,
            "text": "            gdf = gdf[gdf[\"stop_time\"].notna() \u0026 (gdf[\"stop_time\"] \u003e 0)].copy()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 864,
            "text": "        n = len(gdf)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 865,
            "text": "        if n == 0:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 866,
            "text": "            continue"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 867,
            "text": "        promo_rate = (gdf[\"event\"] == 1).sum() / n if \"event\" in gdf.columns else np.nan"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 868,
            "text": "        attr_rate = (gdf[\"event\"] == 2).sum() / n if \"event\" in gdf.columns else np.nan"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 869,
            "text": "        if var_name and var_name in gdf.columns:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 870,
            "text": "            mean_val = pd.to_numeric(gdf[var_name], errors=\"coerce\").mean()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 871,
            "text": "            min_val = pd.to_numeric(gdf[var_name], errors=\"coerce\").min()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 872,
            "text": "            max_val = pd.to_numeric(gdf[var_name], errors=\"coerce\").max()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 873,
            "text": "        else:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 874,
            "text": "            mean_val = min_val = max_val = np.nan"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 875,
            "text": "        group_stats.append({"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 876,
            "text": "            \"group\": group,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 877,
            "text": "            \"n\": n,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 878,
            "text": "            \"promo_rate\": promo_rate,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 879,
            "text": "            \"attr_rate\": attr_rate,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 880,
            "text": "            \"mean_val\": mean_val,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 881,
            "text": "            \"min_val\": min_val,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 882,
            "text": "            \"max_val\": max_val,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 883,
            "text": "        })"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 884
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 885,
            "text": "    if not group_stats:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 886,
            "text": "        print(\"⚠️ No group stats for CIF bar plots.\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 887,
            "text": "        return None"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 888
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 889,
            "text": "    # Sort by mean of variable (ascending). Fallback to group label order."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 1065
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 22,
            "line": 828
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./obsolete_files/cox_plot_helpers_pre_paper.py",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.36420661211013794
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 3451,
            "text": "   \"source\": ["
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3452,
            "text": "    \"# === CELL 10: COMPETING RISKS ANALYSIS (CIF) ===\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3453,
            "text": "    \"# Use custom Cumulative Incidence Function for realistic promotion curves\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3454,
            "text": "    \"\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3455,
            "text": "    \"print(\\\"\\\\n=== COMPETING RISKS ANALYSIS (CIF) ===\\\\n\\\")\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3456,
            "text": "    \"\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3457,
            "text": "    \"# === 10.1. CUSTOM CUMULATIVE INCIDENCE FUNCTION ===\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3458,
            "text": "    \"print(\\\"🔧 Creating custom Cumulative Incidence Function for competing risks...\\\")\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3459,
            "text": "    \"\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3460,
            "text": "    \"def calculate_cumulative_incidence(group_data, event_type, time_col='follow_up_days'):\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3461,
            "text": "    \"    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3462,
            "text": "    \"    Calculate cumulative incidence function for competing risks\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3463,
            "text": "    \"    This properly accounts for competing events and gives realistic rates\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3464,
            "text": "    \"    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3465,
            "text": "    \"    # Get event indicators - handle both old and new data structures\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3466,
            "text": "    \"    if 'competing_event' in group_data.columns:\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3467,
            "text": "    \"        # Old structure (officer-level data)\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3468,
            "text": "    \"        if event_type == 'promotion':\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3469,
            "text": "    \"            event_indicator = (group_data['competing_event'] == 1).astype(int)\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3470,
            "text": "    \"        elif event_type == 'attrition':\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3471,
            "text": "    \"            event_indicator = (group_data['competing_event'] == 2).astype(int)\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3472,
            "text": "    \"    else:\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3473,
            "text": "    \"        # New structure (time-varying survival data)\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3474,
            "text": "    \"        if event_type == 'promotion':\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3475,
            "text": "    \"            event_indicator = (group_data['event'] == 1).astype(int)\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3476,
            "text": "    \"        elif event_type == 'attrition':\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3477,
            "text": "    \"            event_indicator = (group_data['event'] == 2).astype(int)\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3478,
            "text": "    \"    \\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3479,
            "text": "    \"    # Get unique time points\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3480,
            "text": "    \"    times = sorted(group_data[time_col].unique())\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3481,
            "text": "    \"    \\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3482,
            "text": "    \"    # Calculate cumulative incidence\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3483,
            "text": "    \"    cumulative_incidence = []\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3484,
            "text": "    \"    \\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3485,
            "text": "    \"    # Use correct denominator based on data structure\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3486,
            "text": "    \"    if 'competing_event' in group_data.columns:\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3487,
            "text": "    \"        # Officer-level data: use total number of officers\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3488,
            "text": "    \"        total_population = len(group_data)\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3489,
            "text": "    \"    else:\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3490,
            "text": "    \"        # Time-varying data: use total number of UNIQUE officers\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3491,
            "text": "    \"        total_population = group_data['pid_pde'].nunique()\\n\","
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 3491
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 3450
          }

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 752,
            "text": "        \"\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 753,
            "text": "        \"# Create a clean dataset for Cox modeling (exclude non-covariate columns like pid_pde)\\n\","
          },

            "text": "        \"cox_columns = ['start', 'stop', 'event'] + final_covariates\\n\","

          {

          },
          {

          },
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 760,
            "text": "        \"\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 761,
            "text": "        \"# Try to fit the model with available covariates using explicit formula\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 762,
            "text": "        \"try:\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 763,
            "text": "        \"    formula = ' + '.join(final_covariates)\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 764,

          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 766,
            "text": "        \"    \\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 767,
            "text": "        \"    cph.fit(cox_clean_df, \\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 768,
            "text": "        \"            duration_col='stop', \\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 769,
            "text": "        \"            event_col='event',\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 770,
            "text": "        \"            entry_col='start',\\n\","
          },
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 772,
            "text": "        \"    \\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 773,
            "text": "        \"    print(\\\"✅ Cox model fitted successfully!\\\")\\n\","
          },
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 775,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 776,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 777,
            "text": "        \"    print(f\\\"\\\\nModel Performance:\\\")\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 778,
            "text": "        \"    print(f\\\"Concordance Index: {cph.concordance_index_:.3f}\\\")\\n\","
          },
          {

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "## Competing Risks (CR) and Hazard Ratios (HR) for Dummies"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "This note explains how we build and interpret the two main plot types in the pipeline:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "- Competing risks CIF plots (Cell 11)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "- Hazard‑ratio and partial‑effects plots (Cell 12.6)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "It is written for quick re‑orientation, not for formal statistics. The goal is to match the logic of the code you are running in &#96;520_pipeline_cox_working.ipynb&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "## 1) The three core ideas"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "### 1.1 Time is split into intervals"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "We take snapshots of each officer over time. Each snapshot becomes a time interval. The model uses these intervals to learn how things change over time."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "### 1.2 Events happen at the end"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "Promotion or attrition is recorded at a single “event time.” In the data, that is the end of the last interval."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "### 1.3 We handle two event types"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "Promotion and attrition compete with each other. If someone attrites, they can no longer be promoted, and vice‑versa. That is why we use competing‑risks methods."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "### Formal note (core definitions)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "Let $T$ be time‑to‑event and $J$ be event type ($J=1$ promotion, $J=2$ attrition, $J=0$ censored)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "- **Survival function**:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "$$"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "S(t) = P(T \u003e t)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "$$"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "- **Cause‑specific hazard** for event $k$:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "$$"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "h_k(t) = \\lim_{\\Delta t\\to 0} \\frac{P(t \\le T \u003c t+\\Delta t,\\ J=k \\mid T \\ge t)}{\\Delta t}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "$$"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "These are *instantaneous* rates (hazards), not cumulative probabilities."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "## 2) What data rows look like"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "Each snapshot row becomes one survival record interval:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "- &#96;start_time&#96;: days from DOR CPT to the snapshot date."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "- &#96;stop_time&#96;: days from DOR CPT to the next snapshot date."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45,
            "text": "- For the final snapshot:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "  - &#96;stop_time&#96; is the event date (promotion/attrition) or the study end date."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "text": "  - &#96;event&#96; is 1 for promotion, 2 for attrition, 0 for censored."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49,
            "text": "Each row also contains:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "- Static covariates: sex, YG, etc."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51,
            "text": "- Time‑varying covariates: snapshot variables (married, job_code, OER metrics, pool metrics, etc.)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53,
            "text": "This is built in Cell 10."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55,
            "text": "### Formal note (interval construction)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "Each officer $i$ produces intervals $(t_{i,j-1}, t_{i,j}]$ with covariates $X_{i,j}$ observed at the **start** of the interval (snapshot date). The event indicator is attached to the **final** interval only:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58,
            "text": "- $event = 1$ if promoted, $event = 2$ if attrited, $event = 0$ if censored."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 59
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./current_documents/CR_AND_HR_FOR_DUMMIES.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3608737885951996
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 3074,
            "text": "   \"source\": ["
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3075,
            "text": "    \"# === CELL 8: CREATE COMPETING RISKS DATA ===\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3076,
            "text": "    \"# Derive survival variables from dates for competing risks analysis\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3077,
            "text": "    \"\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3078,
            "text": "    \"print(\\\"\\\\n=== CREATE COMPETING RISKS DATA ===\\\\n\\\")\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3079,
            "text": "    \"\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3080,
            "text": "    \"# === 8.1. CREATE OFFICER-LEVEL DATA ===\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3081,
            "text": "    \"print(\\\"🔧 Creating officer-level competing risks data...\\\")\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3082,
            "text": "    \"\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3083,
            "text": "    \"# Get unique officers with their key dates\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3084,
            "text": "    \"officer_data = df_base.groupby('pid_pde').agg({\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3085,
            "text": "    \"    'sex': 'first',\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3086,
            "text": "    \"    'yg': 'first',\\n\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3087,
            "text": "    \"    'age_cpt': 'first',\\n\","

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "# ============================================================================="
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "# CELL 13: Linear probability model (LPM) with quadratic pool quality + season FE"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "# ============================================================================="
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "# Model: Y_draft ~ perf + poolq_loo + poolq_sq + season dummies (drop_first)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "# - Y is binary draft indicator; LPM coefficients are marginal probability changes."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "# - Cluster-robust SEs at team_id account for repeated team-seasons across years/players."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "# - Turning point of quadratic in poolq_loo (holding linear terms fixed): -b2/(2*b3)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "try:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "    import statsmodels.api as sm"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "    HAS_SM = True"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "except ImportError:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "    HAS_SM = False"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "    print(\"Install statsmodels for LPM + clustered SEs: pip install statsmodels\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "if HAS_SM:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "    # Complete-case for all regressors (SR perf can be NaN for unmatched rows)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "    d = g.dropna(subset=[\"perf\", \"poolq_loo\", \"poolq_sq\", \"Y_draft\"]).copy()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "    # Season fixed effects: drop_first avoids perfect collinearity with constant."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "    season_d = pd.get_dummies(d[\"season\"].astype(int), prefix=\"s\", drop_first=True).astype(float)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "    X = pd.concat([d[[\"perf\", \"poolq_loo\", \"poolq_sq\"]].astype(float), season_d], axis=1)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "    X = sm.add_constant(X, has_constant=\"add\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "    y = d[\"Y_draft\"].astype(float)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "    # cov_type=\"cluster\" + groups=team_id: inference robust to within-team correlation."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "    res = sm.OLS(y, X).fit(cov_type=\"cluster\", cov_kwds={\"groups\": d[\"team_id\"]})"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "    print(res.summary().tables[1])"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "    b2 = res.params.get(\"poolq_loo\", float(\"nan\"))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "    b3 = res.params.get(\"poolq_sq\", float(\"nan\"))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "    if np.isfinite(b2) and np.isfinite(b3) and b3 != 0:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "        tp = -b2 / (2 * b3)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "        print(f\"\\nTurning point (poolq): {tp:.4f}\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "else:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "    print(\"Skipping fit — add statsmodels\")"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 45,
            "line": 33
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./_530_extract/cell_13.py",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.293000191450119
    },

            "lineNumber": 57,
            "text": "def run_conductor_from_panel(cfg: Any, panel: pd.DataFrame) -\u003e dict[str, Any]:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59,
            "text": "    Run perf → integrity → coverage → ventiles → export on &#96;&#96;panel&#96;&#96; without reloading it."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61,
            "text": "    &#96;&#96;panel&#96;&#96; must be the pre-perf frame (same as &#96;&#96;prepare_panel&#96;&#96; returns). Pass"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62,
            "text": "    &#96;&#96;panel.copy(deep=True)&#96;&#96; per metric when looping so each run starts from a clean base."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 63,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 64,
            "text": "    out: dict[str, Any] = {\"stage\": \"530_conductor\"}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 65,
            "text": "    out[\"panel_shape_before_perf\"] = tuple(panel.shape)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 67,
            "text": "    panel_work = panel_build.apply_perf_metric_for_analysis("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 68,
            "text": "        panel,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 69,
            "text": "        getattr(cfg, \"perf_metric\", \"minutes\"),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 70,
            "text": "        poolq_winsor_quantiles=getattr(cfg, \"poolq_winsor_quantiles\", None),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 71,
            "text": "        zscore_perf_within_season=bool(getattr(cfg, \"perf_zscore_within_season\", False)),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 72,
            "text": "    )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 73,
            "text": "    out[\"PANEL\"] = panel_work"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 74
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 75,
            "text": "    if bool(getattr(cfg, \"run_data_integrity_report\", True)):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 76,
            "text": "        print(\"--- Data integrity ---\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 77,
            "text": "        data_integrity.print_data_integrity(panel_work, cfg)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 78,
            "text": "        print()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 79
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80,
            "text": "    if bool(getattr(cfg, \"run_yearly_coverage_chart\", True)):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 81,
            "text": "        tab, png = dataset_coverage.export_yearly_coverage_chart(cfg, show=True)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 82,
            "text": "        out[\"coverage_table\"] = tab"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 83,
            "text": "        out[\"coverage_png\"] = png"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 84
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 85,
            "text": "    if bool(getattr(cfg, \"run_ventile_eda\", True)):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86,
            "text": "        bin_tab, png = panel_build.run_ventile_eda(panel_work, cfg)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 87,
            "text": "        out[\"ventile_table\"] = bin_tab"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 88,
            "text": "        out[\"ventile_png\"] = png"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 89
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 90,
            "text": "    if bool(getattr(cfg, \"run_dirty_lpm\", True)):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 91,
            "text": "        lpm = panel_build.run_dirty_lpm(panel_work, cfg)"

            Prepare data for plotting based on plot specification
            Handles time-varying survival interval data
            For Kaplan-Meier plots: Aggregates to officer-level FIRST, then creates groups
            # For Kaplan-Meier plots, we MUST aggregate to officer-level first
            # For competing risks, we also aggregate to officer-level for KM fitting
            # Use last interval for each officer (contains final event and stop_time)

                            # Filter out officers where variable is NaN (no OER data for this specific metric)

        def plot_kaplan_meier(df, plot_spec, save_path, metadata):
            """Create Kaplan-Meier plot using scikit-survival with enhanced formatting"""
            from sksurv.nonparametric import kaplan_meier_estimator
            from sksurv.util import Surv
                valid_mask = (group_data['stop_time'].notna()) &amp; (group_data['stop_time'] &gt; 0)
                # Debug: Check event distribution
                event_counts = officer_data['event'].value_counts().sort_index()
                print_v(f"    Event distribution: {dict(event_counts)}")
                print_v(f"    stop_time range: {officer_data['stop_time'].min():.0f} - {officer_data['stop_time'].max():
                # Create event indicator: True if promoted (event == 1), False otherwise (attrited or censored)
                # For KM, we treat promotion as the event of interest, others as censored
                event_observed = (officer_data['event'] == 1).astype(bool)  # Promotion events
                # Debug: Check promotion events
                n_promoted = event_observed.sum()
                n_total = len(event_observed)
                # Create scikit-survival Surv structure
                y = Surv.from_arrays(
                    event=event_observed,
                    time=officer_data['stop_time'].values
                # Calculate the Kaplan-Meier estimator
                time, survival_prob = kaplan_meier_estimator(y['event'], y['time'])
                # Calculate promotion probability = 1 - survival function
                promotion_prob = 1 - survival_prob
            ax.set_ylim(0, 1)  # Set y-axis limits to match competing risks plots for consistent text positioning
        def calculate_cif(group_data, event_type):
            Calculate Cumulative Incidence Function (CIF) using simple proportion approach
            group_data : DataFrame - Officer-level data with 'stop_time' and 'event' columns
            event_type : int - Event type to calculate CIF for (1=promotion, 2=attrition)
            cif : array - Cumulative incidence values
            # Sort by stop_time
            data = group_data.sort_values('stop_time').copy()
            # Get event indicator
            if event_type == 1:  # Promotion
                event_indicator = (data['event'] == 1)
            elif event_type == 2:  # Attrition
                event_indicator = (data['event'] == 2)
                raise ValueError(f"Invalid event_type: {event_type}. Must be 1 (promotion) or 2 (attrition)")
            times = sorted(data['stop_time'].unique())
                # Count events of type event_type at exactly time t
                events_at_t = ((data['stop_time'] == t) &amp; event_indicator).sum()
                    ci = events_at_t / total_population
                    ci = cumulative_incidence[-1] + (events_at_t / total_population)
        def plot_competing_risks(df, plot_spec, save_path, metadata):
            """Create competing risks plot using simple proportion CIF calculation"""
                promo_palette = plot_spec.get('cif_bar_palette_promo') or ["#d7e6f5", "#1f5aa6"]
                attr_palette = plot_spec.get('cif_bar_palette_attr') or ["#fde2cf", "#c0581a"]

                # Debug: Check final CIF value

                n_promoted = (group_data['event'] == 1).sum()
                print_v(f"  • Group '{group}': Promotion CIF final={final_cif:.3f}, actual rate={n_promoted/n_total:.3f}
                # print("# ACTUAL PLOT COMMAND ax1.plot(times, promotion_cif, ******************************************
                ax1.plot(times, promotion_cif, 
                ax1.set_title(f"Promotion Probability (CIF): {main_title}", fontsize=title_sz, fontweight='bold', pad=22
            # print("# Plot 2: Attrition events (event == 2)******************************************************")

```
def calculate_cif(group_data, event_type):
            """
            Calculate Cumulative Incidence Function (CIF) using simple proportion approach
            Based on 511 implementation - gives realistic rates that match observed proportions
            
            This calculates cumulative incidence as a simple proportion of the total population,
            which is appropriate for officer-level data where we want unconditional probabilities.
            
            Parameters:
            -----------
            group_data : DataFrame - Officer-level data with 'stop_time' and 'event' columns
            event_type : int - Event type to calculate CIF for (1=promotion, 2=attrition)
            
            Returns:
            --------
            times : array - Time points
            cif : array - Cumulative incidence values
            """
            import numpy as np
            
            # Sort by stop_time
            data = group_data.sort_values('stop_time').copy()
            
            # Get event indicator
            if event_type == 1:  # Promotion
                event_indicator = (data['event'] == 1)
            elif event_type == 2:  # Attrition
                event_indicator = (data['event'] == 2)
            else:
                raise ValueError(f"Invalid event_type: {event_type}. Must be 1 (promotion) or 2 (attrition)")
            
            # Get unique time points (sorted)
            times = sorted(data['stop_time'].unique())
            
            # Total population (officers) - this is the key: use total initial population
            total_population = len(data)
            
            if total_population == 0:
                return np.array([]), np.array([])
            
            # Calculate cumulative incidence as simple proportion
            cumulative_incidence = []
            
            for t in times:
                # Count events of type event_type at exactly time t
                events_at_t = ((data['stop_time'] == t) &amp; event_indicator).sum()
                
                # Calculate cumulative incidence as proportion of TOTAL population
                if len(cumulative_incidence) == 0:
                    ci = events_at_t / total_population
                else:
                    ci = cumulative_incidence[-1] + (events_at_t / total_population)
                
                cumulative_incidence.append(ci)
            
            return np.array(times), np.array(cumulative_incidence)
        

```
</details>
</tool-use>

_**Agent (default)**_

Here is how your **talent-pool competing-risks CIF plots** are built in this project.

## The pool variable is not in the CIF “model”

**“Pool Minus Mean SNR Fwd” (or whatever `variable` / binning you chose)** is used only to **split officers into bins** (`plot_group`). Each bin is a separate subsample; **one curve = one bin**.

So for the CIF step, the pool measure is a **stratification / grouping** variable, not a covariate inside the incidence calculation.

## What actually goes into the CIF calculation (per bin)

After `prepare_plot_data`, the notebook collapses to **one row per officer** (last interval: `groupby('pid_pde').last()`), then for **each** talent-pool group it runs `calculate_cif(group_data, event_type)`.

Inside that function, the only data used are:

| Role | Column | Meaning |
|------|--------|--------|
| **Time** | `stop_time` | Follow-up time for that officer (your plots label the axis as time since the CPT anchor, e.g. days from promotion to CPT to end of follow-up / event). |
| **Outcome** | `event` | `1` = promotion (cause of interest for the **promotion** CIF panel), `2` = attrition (for the **attrition** CIF panel), `0` = censored. |

So the “fitting” is **not** a Cox model with pool on the right-hand side. It is a **nonparametric, group-specific step curve** built from **`stop_time` + `event`**, repeated for each pool bin.

The implementation is explicitly a **“simple proportion”** cumulative incidence: at each distinct `stop_time`, it adds `(# of that event type at that time) / (total N in that bin)` to a running sum. That is **not** the full Aalen–Johansen estimator; your own doc notes that Cell 11 CR curves are **empirical CIF-style curves by bin**, while **Cell 12** is where multivariable Cox formulas live.

## How this matches your figure

- **Top panel (promotion CIF):** within each bin, cumulative incidence for **`event == 1`**, over **`stop_time`**.
- **Bottom bars:** summary of **final** promotion (and attrition) incidence by the same groups (see `plot_competing_risks_cif_bars` using `event` and, for ordering/labels, the binned continuous variable when configured).

If you want the **Cox / cause-specific hazard** side (what actually enters $\exp(\beta'X)$), that is a different step (Cell 12 and your memo `current_documents/CR_AND_HR_FOR_DUMMIES.md`), not these stratified CIF plots.

---

1. **Gameplan alignment**: Stage 4 EDA says "binned / ventile draft rate vs pool quality" and "overlay a simple smoother" - check. LPM quadratic is the smoother.

That's **correct** and arguably better for comparing to the quadratic overlay in the same x scale.

3. **X-axis choice**: They plot `poolq_mean` per ventile vs `draft_rate`. Alternative would be bin midpoints or ventile index. Using **mean poolq in bin** is standard for binned scatter / binscatter interpretation: shows where on the covariate scale each group's outcome sits.

4. **LPM overlay range**: The quadratic curve is drawn on `xs = linspace(lo, hi, 200)` where lo/hi are **min and max of bin_tab["poolq_mean"]`**, not the full range of `poolq_loo` in the microdata.

   **Subtle issue**: Ventile boundaries are based on the full distribution of `poolq_loo`, but the **x-coordinates** of the blue points are **within-bin means**. The min/max of those means is **narrower** than the actual data range (means pull toward center). So the orange curve is plotted from min(poolq_mean) to max(poolq_mean) ± pad, which is slightly **inside** the true support of poolq_loo. For a smooth quadratic overlay, it might be slightly better to span `use["poolq_loo"].quantile([0,1])` or the ventile **edge** midpoints — but for visualization this is a minor nuance; the gameplan doesn't require perfection.

5. **LPM vs ventile means**: Coefficients fit on **all player-seasons** (same filter as ventiles). The ventile means are **nonparametric** summaries; they won't lie on the OLS curve unless the model is perfect. Mismatch at extremes is expected — user's plot shows volatility at high poolq.

6. **Independence / clustering**: Player-seasons are not independent (same athlete, same team). Ventile means don't adjust for this. The gameplan acknowledges "cluster-robust SE by player or team if easy" for LPM — the **dirty** LPM may be iid OLS. Shape diagnostics are still valid as **descriptive**; inference statements need clustering.

7. **Y_draft ever-draft on multiple seasons**: If Y_draft is constant across seasons for an athlete, multiple rows count multiple times — this is **person-season** analysis. The gameplan says grain is player-season and Y_draft ever-draft v0. So a drafted player's college seasons all get Y=1. That's intentional for this v0 but **amplifies** draftees in the panel vs "first season only" — user should know the estimand is player-season draft rate, not unique players drafted.

I'll mention duplicates="drop" if many ties in poolq.

I'll give a clear verdict: **substantively yes, properly constructed** for the agreed gameplan and a reasonable analog to the stratified bar/cross-group comparison in Army CR plots, with caveats on clustering, Y_draft grain, LPM x-range, and rare-qcut bin collapse.

_**Agent (default)**_

Here is a concise assessment after reading `SPORTS_DATA_GAMEPLAN.md`, `sports_pipeline/panel_build.py`, `conductor.py`, and how `530_sports_pipeline.ipynb` wires `PipelineConfig` into that path.

## Verdict

**Yes — for Stage 4 EDA you are building these curves in a principled way** and they are a fair **visual analog** to the Army idea “stratify by pool quality, plot outcome within each stratum,” not a statistical clone of CIF over time.

What you are doing matches the gameplan: *“Ventiles or bins … within each bin, compute the mean or share of `Y_draft` … overlay a simple smoother”* (fast-path checklist and Stage 4 table in `SPORTS_DATA_GAMEPLAN.md`).

---

## What the plot is actually doing (and why it is coherent)

1. **`poolq_loo`** — Leave-one-out mean teammate `perf` within `(team_id, season)`, with optional within-season z-score of `perf` first (`recompute_teammate_loo_pool_quality` + `standardize_perf_zscore_by_season` in `panel_build.py`). Same structure as your replication memo’s pool-quality construct.

2. **Ventiles** — `pd.qcut` on `poolq_loo` with `duplicates="drop"` when needed (`assign_poolq_bin_labels`), i.e. ~equal **counts** per bin when the distribution allows — aligned with your notebook comment that this matches 520-style **quantile** binning.

3. **Blue curve** — For each bin, you aggregate  
   - `draft_rate = mean(Y_draft)`  
   - `poolq_mean = mean(poolq_loo)`  
   (`ventile_table`). You then plot **`poolq_mean` vs `draft_rate`** (`ventile_plot`). That is the standard way to make a **binned outcome vs covariate** plot: the x position is where that bin sits on the pool-quality scale, not an arbitrary index. Connecting points is the line equivalent of **bar heights** at those x positions.

4. **Orange dashed curve** — Same filtered sample as the bins: OLS LPM `Y_draft ~ 1 + poolq_loo + poolq_sq` (`run_dirty_lpm` / `draft_poolq_quadratic_coeffs`), evaluated on a grid; values are clipped to `[0, 1]`. That is a reasonable **parametric** summary of curvature for a binary outcome (gameplan: quadratic / inverted-U read).

So the **logic** “bin on pool quality → mean draft rate in bin → smooth overlay from microdata regression” is implemented consistently.

---

## Caveats (not “wrong,” but know what you are estimating)

| Topic | Note |
|--------|------|
| **vs Army CIF bars** | Army bars summarize **time-to-event** cumulative incidence by pool bin; you summarize **cross-sectional draft incidence** by pool bin. Same *stratify-then-summarize* spirit, different estimand. |
| **Player-season grain** | With ever-draft `Y_draft`, the same athlete can contribute multiple seasons with `Y=1`. Draft **rate** in the plot is per **player-season**, not per unique player — fine if that is your v0 definition; say so in text. |
| **Dependence** | Rows cluster by athlete and team. The **ventile means** are descriptive; **inference** from the LPM should eventually use clustered SEs (gameplan already flags this). Shape diagnostics are still useful. |
| **LPM x-range** | The overlay grid uses `min`/`max` of **ventile `poolq_mean` values**, not the full min/max of `poolq_loo` in the microdata. Slightly tighter than the raw support; minor display choice, not a bug. |
| **`qcut` + ties** | `duplicates="drop"` can yield **fewer** than 20 bins; the CSV/figure still cohere, but the title “20 ventiles” is only exact when you actually get 20 bins. |

---

## Bottom line

You are **not** reproducing competing-risks **CIF fitting** in college (there is no promotion clock or competing exit in this EDA). Here is a tight way to think about it and what you can say back.

## Short answer

**College:** Individual values **do** drive the ventile / “bar chart” picture, but in a specific way: each **player–season** is placed in a bin using **its own** `poolq_loo`. Those bin assignments come from **row-level** pool-quality measures. What is **not** in **that** pool measure is the player’s **own** performance: `poolq_loo` is explicitly **leave-one-out** on teammate `perf`, so the individual’s own TB / `perf` is **not** averaged into **their** pool line (it still affects **teammates’** poolq).

**Army-style pool plot:** Same **type** of thing for the CR figure: the curve or bar group is chosen using **each officer’s** realized pool-related covariate (e.g. pool minus mean, forward-painted). The **CIF mechanics** inside each bin use time and event type; they do not “feed TB into the estimator” beyond **which bin** that officer belongs to.

So if the advisor means *“Does each person’s own TB / performance enter the definition of their pool-quality variable?”* — **on the college side, no for own perf in own poolq (LOO).** If they mean *“Are we using individual-level pool measures to sort people into bins before we aggregate?”* — **yes.**

---

## Slightly longer (for your advisor)

1. **Construction of pool quality (college)**  
   For each row, `poolq_loo` = mean of **teammates’** `perf` on the same `(team_id, season)`, excluding self. So **individual teammate performances** (aggregated across others on the roster) **are** what build the pool; **your** own TB/`perf` is **excluded** from **your** poolq by construction.

2. **What the ventile / draft-rate plot uses**  
   You bin on **each observation’s** `poolq_loo`, then within each bin you plot mean `Y_draft`. So **every player–season’s individual `poolq_loo`** determines which bin it contributes to. That **is** individual-level information going into **stratification**; it is not “only team averages plotted once per team.”

3. **Contrast with the baseline LPM on the same figure**  
   The dashed quadratic is `E[draft|poolq] ≈ β₀ + β₁·poolq + β₂·poolq²` on the micro sample. **Own** `perf` is **not** included as a regressor in that baseline spec unless you add it. So if the question is *“Is own TB in the regression?”* — **not in the default EDA LPM**; if the question is *“Is individual variation in pool quality used?”* — **yes**, via `poolq_loo` on every row.

4. **Parallel to Army “Pool minus mean … fwd”**  
   There too, **each officer** typically carries an **individual** value of that constructed pool signal (with whatever painting rule you use). Bins/groups are defined from those **individual** values; the competing-risk **curve within a bin** is still based on survival outcomes, not on re-feeding TB into the CIF formula.

## Army plots (only this construct)

- The stratifier for each officer is **that leave-self-out pool measure** (built from **other** people’s contributions to the pool).
- **Your own TB is not an input to your own stratifying value** by construction—the mean is over the pool **excluding you**.

## College plots (`poolq_loo`)

## Direct answer to the “only used to bin?” question

With **Army = pool mean without own contribution** and **College = LOO teammate pool**:

_**Agent (default)**_

Here is a cleaner way to say it, and one important fix to how you describe the bar height.

## 1. Are you binned by “pool means” (leave-self-out)?

**Yes, in spirit:** the stratifier is **peer pool quality with your own contribution left out of the pool average** — Army under your definition, college as `poolq_loo`.

## 2. Do both “track a changing pool mean over time”?

**Not automatically the same way in the code you’ve been using.**

- **College (`530`):** Each **player–season** row usually has **`poolq_loo` for that team–season (that year)**. Ventiles bin **that row** using **that year’s** leave-one-out teammate mean — not, by default, a **career-long average** over four years unless you explicitly build and bin on such an average.

- **Army (`520` CR / ventile-style plots):** Pool can be **time-varying** in the survival panel, but the **plot** typically collapses to **one row per officer** for KM/CR and uses the **pool measure carried on that row** (often aligned to a **last snapshot** or similar rule in `prepare_plot_data`). That may or may not equal a **longitudinal average** of pool over a career — it is whatever your pipeline **actually** attaches to the officer for that figure. So: **only say “longitudinal average” if your Army code defines the binning variable that way.**

So: **both can allow pool to change over time in principle**, but **college ventiles as implemented are usually “pool in that season,”** and **Army bars are “whatever single pool value you assigned that officer for that plot,”** which you should name precisely in the paper.

## 3. Bar height / scatter dot height = “my” probability?

**No — that is the main slip.**

- **Army final CIF (or promotion share) in bin 4** = **among everyone who was classified into bin 4**, the **estimated fraction** who experienced promotion by the end of follow-up (under your estimator). It is a **group** summary, not a personal forecast for one officer.

- **College draft rate in ventile 4** = **among all player–seasons in that ventile**, the **sample mean** of `Y_draft` (draft rate in that bin). Again **group**, not “your” probability unless you fit a model that outputs individual predictions.

So you should **not** say: “the height of bin 4 **is** my probability of promotion / making the pros.”

You **can** say: **“If I’m in bin 4, the bar shows the promotion (or draft) rate **observed in the data** for people in bin 4 — an empirical **group** rate, not my individual probability unless we estimate a separate individual-level model.”**

## 4. Tighter wording you can use

- **Army:** “Officers are assigned to a bin using **[define: e.g. leave-self-out pool mean at last pre-event snapshot / or longitudinal average if that’s what you coded]**. The bar for that bin is the **sample promotion incidence** in that bin (under the CR display you use).”

- **College:** “Each **player–season** is assigned to a ventile using **that season’s** leave-one-out teammate **`perf`** mean. The point height is the **draft rate in that ventile** across **player–seasons** in the sample.”

That keeps **pool-without-self** aligned across venues, **time** honest (season vs whatever Army attaches), and **bar height** correctly described as a **group** empirical rate, not “your” probability.

---

### Plan (steps 1–3)

- **VECTOR / SCOUT / you:** Your sequencing is clear: sync docs → swap Coda ↔ Scout → Vector drafts the Brian note after merging both threads.
- **Scout** still needs to produce the **mirror** summary (basketball: ESPN box, draft register, `poolq_loo`, ppm, z-score, winsor, min minutes, team filter, 2011–2021, 8 bins / ventiles, figure interpretation). I can’t run Scout’s notebook from here; the new Coda file **asks Scout explicitly** for that technical paragraph.
- **VECTOR** should merge **`Coda_Vector_Brief_Army_Evidence_For_Brian_Memo.md`** with Scout’s companion brief, then shape voice for Brian MacDonald.

---

### Step 2b — Resubmit the old Coda handoff?

**Don’t replace it; add these.**

- **`Army_to_College_Basketball_Replication_Handoff.md`** and **`Agent_Read_First_Coda_Runbook.md`** stay useful for **pipeline history, Cox/CR detail, and replication design**.
- For **“we got the shape”** and **cross-agent alignment**, use the **new** files below as the **current** narrative.

---

1. **`current_documents/Coda_Summary_For_Scout_and_Vector_Post_Replication.md`**  
   Army setting, **pool = mean without self**, competing-risks **final CIF** bars (Q1–Q8 story), code map, recommendation for Scout’s mirror content, pointer to tenure as a **third** design.

2. **`current_documents/Coda_Vector_Brief_Army_Evidence_For_Brian_Memo.md`**  
   **Vector-facing:** draft **second-paragraph** skeleton (Army only), **interpretation** bullets for paragraph three, lit-check reminder, tenure pointer, merge instructions with Scout.

---

Once **Scout’s** doc exists, Vector should treat **Coda’s Vector brief + Scout’s brief** as the combined package. If you want a **single** merged “Vector master brief” after Scout writes theirs, you can ask **Vector** (or me as Coda in a follow-up) to stitch them into one file.

---

Congratulations again on lining up the Army and college figures—that’s a strong story for Brian and for the paper arc. When Scout’s writeup is ready, paste or path it here if you want Coda to **cross-check** wording against the Army side before Vector sends anything.

### What `Scout_background_CBB_pool_quality_draft_rate.md` is

Scout’s **one-page college side** for Coda, Vector, and Brian: research question aligned to Army; **`Y_draft`** and **`poolq_loo`** (LOO teammate **`perf`**); **ppm** from ESPN box; data sources; **table** of choices (2011–2021, `min_minutes=1`, no team-draftee filter, **8 equal-width** bins, winsor 5–95%); **~83k / ~45k / ~1.1k** draft rows; **hump + drop in bin 8**; reproducibility (`530` + `sports_pipeline/`, export filenames).

### What I changed

The old **“What Scout needs from CODA”** block read like an open to-do. I replaced it with **pointers** to Coda’s post-replication docs and **`Vector_Browser_Package_FULL_TEXT.md`**, plus a one-line **parallel claim** so Vector isn’t left looking for missing Army text.

If you meant something else (e.g. expand z-score, or 12-bin vs 8-bin runs), say what to add and I’ll edit the table.
